query:
You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.175 (commit: 3f67d1d8).

## Issue to Resolve

Add option to Redo Answer Card
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Sometimes, I accidentally click on the Undo button in the reviewer, which undoes the rating of the last card I reviewed.
This happens especially when I am editing a particular card during review because the location of the ✓ button of the edit note window coincides with the location of the undo button.

Because AnkiDroid doesn't have the Redo button, I have to rethink which answer button should be used for rating that card.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
This problem can be solved by adding a Redo option, just like Desktop Anki has.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

**Additional context**
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Really liked the feature request. I'd try to implement it  
I've been working a bit on implementing this feature and had some questions for other contributers. Would a redo button like this also stay active while the whiteboard is up to redo strokes? I believe that would make the most sense, but I want to see if anyone has any objections. 
So, as of #14171, general redo suport should be practical to implement. The whiteboard is another matter, and may need its own undo/redo buttons.
Im gonna do it

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/Reviewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/Undo.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/ViewerCommand.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/preferences/CustomButtonsSettingsFragment.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/reviewer/ActionButtonStatus.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Collection.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/ui/RtlCompliantActionProvider.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/drawable/ic_redo.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/menu/reviewer.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/01-core.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/preferences.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_controls.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_custom_buttons.xml`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

17→package com.ichi2.anki
18→
19→import androidx.fragment.app.FragmentActivity
20→import anki.collection.OpChangesAfterUndo
21→import com.google.android.material.snackbar.Snackbar
22→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.TR
23→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
24→import com.ichi2.libanki.undo
25→import com.ichi2.libanki.undoableOp
26→
27→/** If there's an action pending in the review queue, undo it and show a snackbar */
28→suspend fun FragmentActivity.undoAndShowSnackbar(duration: Int = Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT) {
29→    withProgress {
30→        val changes = undoableOp {
31→            if (!undoAvailable()) {
32→                OpChangesAfterUndo.getDefaultInstance()
33→            } else {
34→                undo()
35→            }
36→        }
37→        val message = if (changes.operation.isEmpty()) {
38→            TR.actionsNothingToUndo()
39→        } else {
40→            TR.undoActionUndone(changes.operation)
41→        }
42→        showSnackbar(message, duration)
43→    }
44→}
45→

 50→// Anki maintains a cache of used tags so it can quickly present a list of tags
 51→// for autocomplete and in the browser. For efficiency, deletions are not
 52→// tracked, so unused tags can only be removed from the list with a DB check.

183→    fun schedVer(): Int {

193→    fun _loadScheduler() {
194→        val ver = schedVer()
195→        if (ver == 1) {

197→        } else {

199→                backend.setConfigBool(ConfigKey.Bool.SCHED_2021, true, undoable = false)

212→    fun close(downgrade: Boolean = false, forFullSync: Boolean = false) {
213→        if (!dbClosed) {
214→            if (!forFullSync) {

226→        return if (dbClosed) {
227→            val (db_, created) = Storage.openDB(path, backend, afterFullSync)
228→            dbInternal = db_
229→            load()
230→            _openLog()
231→            if (afterFullSync) {
232→                _loadScheduler()
233→            }
234→            created

236→            false
237→        }

249→     * is used so that the type does not states that an exception is
250→     * thrown when in fact it is never thrown.

252→    open fun modSchemaNoCheck() {

260→    /** Mark schema modified to force a full sync.
261→     * ConfirmModSchemaException will be thrown if the user needs to be prompted to confirm the action.
262→     * If the user chooses to confirm then modSchemaNoCheck should be called, after which the exception can
263→     * be safely ignored, and the outer code called again.
264→     *
265→     * @throws ConfirmModSchemaException
266→     */
267→    @Throws(ConfirmModSchemaException::class)
268→    fun modSchema() {
269→        if (!schemaChanged()) {
270→            /* In Android we can't show a dialog which blocks the main UI thread
271→             Therefore we can't wait for the user to confirm if they want to do
272→             a full sync here, and we instead throw an exception asking the outer
273→             code to handle the user's choice */
274→            throw ConfirmModSchemaException()
275→        }
276→        modSchemaNoCheck()
277→    }

422→        // This function shouldn't exist and CardBrowser should be modified to use Notes,
423→        // so not much effort was expended here
424→
425→        val noteIds = findNotes(query, SortOrder.NoOrdering())
426→        // select the card with the lowest `ord` to show
427→        return db.queryLongList(
428→            """
429→SELECT c.id
430→FROM (
431→  SELECT nid, MIN(ord) AS ord
432→  FROM cards
433→  WHERE nid IN ${Utils.ids2str(noteIds)} 
434→  GROUP BY nid
435→) AS card_with_min_ord
436→JOIN cards AS c ON card_with_min_ord.nid = c.nid AND card_with_min_ord.ord = c.ord;
437→            """.trimMargin()
438→        )
439→    }

457→    fun startTimebox() {

459→        mStartReps = sched.reps

461→
462→    data class TimeboxReached(val secs: Int, val reps: Int)
463→
464→    /* Return (elapsedTime, reps) if timebox reached, or null.
465→    * Automatically restarts timebox if expired. */
466→    fun timeboxReached(): TimeboxReached? {
467→        if (sched.timeboxSecs() == 0) {
468→            // timeboxing disabled
469→            return null
470→        }
471→        val elapsed = TimeManager.time.intTime() - mStartTime
472→        val limit = sched.timeboxSecs()
473→        return if (elapsed > limit) {
474→            TimeboxReached(
475→                limit,
476→                sched.reps - mStartReps
477→            ).also {
478→                startTimebox()
479→            }
480→        } else {
481→            null
482→        }
483→    }

536→    /*
537→     * Basic integrity check. Only used by unit tests.
538→     */
539→    @KotlinCleanup("have getIds() return a list of mids and define idsToStr over it")
540→    fun basicCheck(): Boolean {
541→        // cards without notes
542→        if (db.queryScalar("select 1 from cards where nid not in (select id from notes) limit 1") > 0) {
543→            return false
544→        }
545→        val badNotes = db.queryScalar(
546→            "select 1 from notes where id not in (select distinct nid from cards) " +
547→                "or mid not in " + Utils.ids2str(notetypes.ids()) + " limit 1"
548→        ) > 0
549→        // notes without cards or models
550→        if (badNotes) {
551→            return false
552→        }
553→        // invalid ords
554→        for (m in notetypes.all()) {
555→            // ignore clozes
556→            if (m.getInt("type") != Consts.MODEL_STD) {
557→                continue
558→            }
559→            // Make a list of valid ords for this model
560→            val tmpls = m.getJSONArray("tmpls")
561→            val badOrd = db.queryScalar(
562→                "select 1 from cards where (ord < 0 or ord >= ?) and nid in ( " +
563→                    "select id from notes where mid = ?) limit 1",
564→                tmpls.length(),
565→                m.getLong("id")
566→            ) > 0
567→            if (badOrd) {
568→                return false
569→            }
570→        }
571→        return true
572→    }

574→    fun log(vararg objects: Any?) {
575→        if (!debugLog) return
576→
577→        val unixTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
578→
579→        val outerTraceElement = Thread.currentThread().stackTrace[3]
580→        val fileName = outerTraceElement.fileName
581→        val methodName = outerTraceElement.methodName
582→
583→        val objectsString = objects
584→            .map { if (it is LongArray) Arrays.toString(it) else it }
585→            .joinToString(", ")
586→
587→        writeLog("[$unixTime] $fileName:$methodName() $objectsString")
588→    }
589→
590→    private fun writeLog(s: String) {
591→        mLogHnd?.let {
592→            try {
593→                it.println(s)
594→            } catch (e: Exception) {
595→                Timber.w(e, "Failed to write to collection log")
596→            }
597→        }
598→        Timber.d(s)
599→    }
600→
601→    private fun _openLog() {
602→        Timber.i("Opening Collection Log")
603→        if (!debugLog) {
604→            return
605→        }
606→        try {
607→            val lpath = File(path.replaceFirst("\\.anki2$".toRegex(), ".log"))
608→            if (lpath.exists() && lpath.length() > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
609→                val lpath2 = File("$lpath.old")
610→                if (lpath2.exists()) {
611→                    lpath2.delete()
612→                }
613→                lpath.renameTo(lpath2)
614→            }
615→            mLogHnd = PrintWriter(BufferedWriter(FileWriter(lpath, true)), true)
616→        } catch (e: IOException) {
617→            // turn off logging if we can't open the log file
618→            Timber.e("Failed to open collection.log file - disabling logging")
619→            debugLog = false
620→        }
621→    }
622→
623→    private fun _closeLog() {
624→        Timber.i("Closing Collection Log")
625→        mLogHnd?.close()
626→        mLogHnd = null
627→    }

19→import anki.collection.OpChangesAfterUndo
20→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.RustCleanup
21→import anki.collection.UndoStatus as UndoStatusProto
22→
23→/**
24→ * If undo/redo available, a localized string describing the action will be set.
25→ */
26→data class UndoStatus(
27→    val undo: String?,
28→    val redo: String?,
29→    // not currently used
30→    val lastStep: Int
31→) {
32→    companion object {
33→        fun from(proto: UndoStatusProto): UndoStatus {
34→            return UndoStatus(
35→                undo = proto.undo.ifEmpty { null },
36→                redo = proto.redo.ifEmpty { null },
37→                lastStep = proto.lastStep
38→            )
39→        }
40→    }
41→}
42→
43→/**
44→ * Undo the last backend operation.
45→ *
46→ * Should be called via collection.op(), which will notify
47→ * [ChangeManager.Subscriber] of the changes.
48→ *
49→ * Will throw if no undo operation is possible (due to legacy code
50→ * directly mutating the database).
51→ */
52→@RustCleanup("Once fully migrated, and v2 scheduler dropped, rename to undo()")
53→fun Collection.undo(): OpChangesAfterUndo {
54→    return backend.undo()
55→}
56→
57→/** Redoes the previously-undone operation. See the docs for
58→[Collection.undoOperation]
59→ */
60→fun Collection.redo(): OpChangesAfterUndo {
61→    return backend.redo()
62→}
63→
64→/** See [UndoStatus] */
65→fun Collection.undoStatus(): UndoStatus {
66→    return UndoStatus.from(backend.getUndoStatus())
67→}
68→

  95→open class Reviewer :
  96→    AbstractFlashcardViewer(),
  97→    ReviewerUi {
  98→    var queueState: CurrentQueueState? = null
  99→    val customSchedulingKey = TimeManager.time.intTimeMS().toString()
 100→    private var mHasDrawerSwipeConflicts = false
 101→    private var mShowWhiteboard = true
 102→    private var mPrefFullscreenReview = false
 103→    private lateinit var mColorPalette: LinearLayout
 104→    private var toggleStylus = false
 105→
 106→    // TODO: Consider extracting to ViewModel
 107→    // Card counts
 108→    private var mNewCount: SpannableString? = null
 109→    private var mLrnCount: SpannableString? = null
 110→    private var mRevCount: SpannableString? = null
 111→    private lateinit var mTextBarNew: TextView
 112→    private lateinit var mTextBarLearn: TextView
 113→    private lateinit var mTextBarReview: TextView
 114→    private lateinit var answerTimer: AnswerTimer
 115→    private var mPrefHideDueCount = false
 116→
 117→    // Whiteboard
 118→    var prefWhiteboard = false
 119→
 120→    @get:CheckResult
 121→    @get:VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
 122→    var whiteboard: Whiteboard? = null
 123→        protected set
 124→
 125→    // Record Audio
 126→    /** File of the temporary mic record  */
 127→    @get:VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
 128→    var audioView: AudioView? = null
 129→        protected set
 130→    private var tempAudioPath: String? = null
 131→
 132→    // ETA
 133→    private var mEta = 0
 134→    private var mPrefShowETA = false
 135→
 136→    /** Handle Mark/Flag state of cards  */
 137→    private var mCardMarker: CardMarker? = null
 138→
 139→    // Preferences from the collection
 140→    private var mShowRemainingCardCount = false
 141→    private val mActionButtons = ActionButtons(this)
 142→    private lateinit var mToolbar: Toolbar
 143→
 144→    @VisibleForTesting
 145→    protected val mProcessor = PeripheralKeymap(this, this)
 146→    private val mOnboarding = Onboarding.Reviewer(this)
 147→
 148→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 149→        if (showedActivityFailedScreen(savedInstanceState)) {
 150→            return
 151→        }
 152→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 153→        if (handledLaunchFromWebBrowser(intent, this)) {
 154→            this.setResult(RESULT_CANCELED)
 155→            finishWithAnimation(ActivityTransitionAnimation.Direction.END)
 156→            return
 157→        }
 158→        mColorPalette = findViewById(R.id.whiteboard_editor)
 159→        answerTimer = AnswerTimer(findViewById(R.id.card_time))
 160→        mTextBarNew = findViewById(R.id.new_number)
 161→        mTextBarLearn = findViewById(R.id.learn_number)
 162→        mTextBarReview = findViewById(R.id.review_number)
 163→        mToolbar = findViewById(R.id.toolbar)
 164→
 165→        startLoadingCollection()
 166→    }
 167→
 168→    override fun onPause() {
 169→        answerTimer.pause()
 170→        super.onPause()
 171→    }
 172→
 173→    override fun onResume() {
 174→        answerTimer.resume()
 175→        super.onResume()
 176→        if (answerField != null) {
 177→            answerField!!.focusWithKeyboard()
 178→        }
 179→    }
 180→
 181→    protected val flagToDisplay: Int
 182→        get() {
 183→            return FlagToDisplay(
 184→                currentCard!!.userFlag(),
 185→                mActionButtons.findMenuItem(ActionButtons.RES_FLAG)?.isActionButton ?: true,
 186→                mPrefFullscreenReview
 187→            ).get()
 188→        }
 189→
 190→    override fun recreateWebView() {
 191→        super.recreateWebView()
 192→        setRenderWorkaround(this)
 193→    }
 194→
 195→    @NeedsTest("is hidden if marked is on app bar")
 196→    @NeedsTest("is not hidden if marked is not on app bar")
 197→    @NeedsTest("is not hidden if marked is on app bar and fullscreen is enabled")
 198→    override fun shouldDisplayMark(): Boolean {
 199→        val markValue = super.shouldDisplayMark()
 200→        if (!markValue) {
 201→            return false
 202→        }
 203→
 204→        // If we don't know: assume it's not shown
 205→        val shownAsToolbarButton = mActionButtons.findMenuItem(ActionButtons.RES_MARK)?.isActionButton == true
 206→        return !shownAsToolbarButton || mPrefFullscreenReview
 207→    }
 208→
 209→    protected open fun onMark(card: Card?) {
 210→        if (card == null) {
 211→            return
 212→        }
 213→        launchCatchingTask {
 214→            toggleMark(card.note())
 215→            refreshActionBar()
 216→            onMarkChanged()
 217→        }
 218→    }
 219→
 220→    private fun onMarkChanged() {
 221→        if (currentCard == null) {
 222→            return
 223→        }
 224→        mCardMarker!!.displayMark(shouldDisplayMark())
 225→    }
 226→
 227→    protected open fun onFlag(card: Card?, flag: Int) {
 228→        if (card == null) {
 229→            return
 230→        }
 231→        launchCatchingTask {
 232→            card.setUserFlag(flag)
 233→            withCol {
 234→                setUserFlagForCards(listOf(card.id), flag)
 235→            }
 236→            refreshActionBar()
 237→            onFlagChanged()
 238→        }
 239→    }
 240→
 241→    private fun onFlagChanged() {
 242→        if (currentCard == null) {
 243→            return
 244→        }
 245→        mCardMarker!!.displayFlag(flagToDisplay)
 246→    }
 247→
 248→    private fun selectDeckFromExtra() {
 249→        val extras = intent.extras
 250→        if (extras == null || !extras.containsKey("deckId")) {
 251→            // deckId is not set, load default
 252→            return
 253→        }
 254→        val did = extras.getLong("deckId", Long.MIN_VALUE)
 255→        Timber.d("selectDeckFromExtra() with deckId = %d", did)
 256→
 257→        // deckId does not exist, load default
 258→        if (getColUnsafe.decks.get(did) == null) {
 259→            Timber.w("selectDeckFromExtra() deckId '%d' doesn't exist", did)
 260→            return
 261→        }
 262→        // Select the deck
 263→        getColUnsafe.decks.select(did)
 264→    }
 265→
 266→    override fun getContentViewAttr(fullscreenMode: FullScreenMode): Int {
 267→        return when (fullscreenMode) {
 268→            FullScreenMode.BUTTONS_ONLY -> R.layout.reviewer_fullscreen
 269→            FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE -> R.layout.reviewer_fullscreen_noanswers
 270→            else -> R.layout.reviewer
 271→        }
 272→    }
 273→
 274→    public override fun fitsSystemWindows(): Boolean {
 275→        return !fullscreenMode.isFullScreenReview()
 276→    }
 277→
 278→    override fun onCollectionLoaded(col: Collection) {
 279→        super.onCollectionLoaded(col)
 280→        if (Intent.ACTION_VIEW == intent.action) {
 281→            Timber.d("onCreate() :: received Intent with action = %s", intent.action)
 282→            selectDeckFromExtra()
 283→        }
 284→        // Load the first card and start reviewing. Uses the answer card
 285→        // task to load a card, but since we send null
 286→        // as the card to answer, no card will be answered.
 287→        prefWhiteboard = MetaDB.getWhiteboardState(this, parentDid)
 288→        if (prefWhiteboard) {
 289→            // DEFECT: Slight inefficiency here, as we set the database using these methods
 290→            val whiteboardVisibility = MetaDB.getWhiteboardVisibility(this, parentDid)
 291→            setWhiteboardEnabledState(true)
 292→            setWhiteboardVisibility(whiteboardVisibility)
 293→            toggleStylus = MetaDB.getWhiteboardStylusState(this, parentDid)
 294→            whiteboard!!.toggleStylus = toggleStylus
 295→        }
 296→        launchCatchingTask { updateCardAndRedraw() }
 297→        disableDrawerSwipeOnConflicts()
 298→
 299→        // Set full screen/immersive mode if needed
 300→        if (mPrefFullscreenReview) {
 301→            setFullScreen(this)
 302→        }
 303→        setRenderWorkaround(this)
 304→    }
 305→
 306→    override fun onOptionsItemSelected(item: MenuItem): Boolean {
 307→        // 100ms was not enough on my device (Honor 9 Lite -  Android Pie)
 308→        delayedHide(1000)
 309→        if (drawerToggle.onOptionsItemSelected(item)) {
 310→            return true
 311→        }
 312→        when (item.itemId) {
 313→            android.R.id.home -> {
 314→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Home button pressed")
 315→                closeReviewer(RESULT_OK)
 316→            }
 317→            R.id.action_undo -> {
 318→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Undo button pressed")
 319→                if (mShowWhiteboard && whiteboard != null && !whiteboard!!.undoEmpty()) {
 320→                    whiteboard!!.undo()
 321→                } else {
 322→                    undo()
 323→                }
 324→            }
 325→            R.id.action_reset_card_progress -> {
 326→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Reset progress button pressed")
 327→                showResetCardDialog()
 328→            }
 329→            R.id.action_mark_card -> {
 330→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Mark button pressed")
 331→                onMark(currentCard)
 332→            }
 333→            R.id.action_replay -> {
 334→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Replay audio button pressed (from menu)")
 335→                playSounds(true)
 336→            }
 337→            R.id.action_toggle_mic_tool_bar -> {
 338→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Record mic")
 339→                // Check permission to record and request if not granted
 340→                openOrToggleMicToolbar()
 341→            }
 342→            R.id.action_tag -> {
 343→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Tag button pressed")
 344→                showTagsDialog()
 345→            }
 346→            R.id.action_edit -> {
 347→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Edit note button pressed")
 348→                editCard()
 349→            }
 350→            R.id.action_bury -> {
 351→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Bury button pressed")
 352→                MenuItemCompat.getActionProvider(item)?.hasSubMenu()?.let { isAvailable ->
 353→                    if (!isAvailable) {
 354→                        Timber.d("Bury card due to no submenu")
 355→                        buryCard()
 356→                    }
 357→                } ?: Timber.w("Null ActionProvider for bury menu item in Reviewer!")
 358→            }
 359→            R.id.action_suspend -> {
 360→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Suspend button pressed")
 361→                MenuItemCompat.getActionProvider(item)?.hasSubMenu()?.let { isAvailable ->
 362→                    if (!isAvailable) {
 363→                        Timber.d("Suspend card due to no submenu")
 364→                        suspendCard()
 365→                    }
 366→                } ?: Timber.w("Null ActionProvider for suspend menu item in Reviewer!")
 367→            }
 368→            R.id.action_delete -> {
 369→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Delete note button pressed")
 370→                showDeleteNoteDialog()
 371→            }
 372→            R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color -> {
 373→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Pen Color button pressed")
 374→                changeWhiteboardPenColor()
 375→            }
 376→            R.id.action_save_whiteboard -> {
 377→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Save whiteboard button pressed")
 378→                if (whiteboard != null) {
 379→                    try {
 380→                        val savedWhiteboardFileName = whiteboard!!.saveWhiteboard(TimeManager.time).path
 381→                        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.white_board_image_saved, savedWhiteboardFileName), Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT)
 382→                    } catch (e: Exception) {
 383→                        Timber.w(e)
 384→                        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.white_board_image_save_failed, e.localizedMessage), Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT)
 385→                    }
 386→                }
 387→            }
 388→            R.id.action_clear_whiteboard -> {
 389→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Clear whiteboard button pressed")
 390→                clearWhiteboard()
 391→            }
 392→            R.id.action_hide_whiteboard -> { // toggle whiteboard visibility
 393→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Whiteboard visibility set to %b", !mShowWhiteboard)
 394→                setWhiteboardVisibility(!mShowWhiteboard)
 395→                refreshActionBar()
 396→            }
 397→            R.id.action_toggle_stylus -> { // toggle stylus mode
 398→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Stylus set to %b", !toggleStylus)
 399→                toggleStylus = !toggleStylus
 400→                whiteboard!!.toggleStylus = toggleStylus
 401→                MetaDB.storeWhiteboardStylusState(this, parentDid, toggleStylus)
 402→                refreshActionBar()
 403→            }
 404→            R.id.action_toggle_whiteboard -> {
 405→                toggleWhiteboard()
 406→            }
 407→            R.id.action_open_deck_options -> {
 408→                val i = com.ichi2.anki.pages.DeckOptions.getIntent(this, getColUnsafe.decks.current().id)
 409→                deckOptionsLauncher.launch(i)
 410→            }
 411→            R.id.action_select_tts -> {
 412→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Select TTS button pressed")
 413→                showSelectTtsDialogue()
 414→            }
 415→            R.id.action_add_note_reviewer -> {
 416→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Add note button pressed")
 417→                addNote()
 418→            }
 419→            R.id.action_flag_zero -> {
 420→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: No flag")
 421→                onFlag(currentCard, CardMarker.FLAG_NONE)
 422→            }
 423→            R.id.action_flag_one -> {
 424→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Flag one")
 425→                onFlag(currentCard, CardMarker.FLAG_RED)
 426→            }
 427→            R.id.action_flag_two -> {
 428→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Flag two")
 429→                onFlag(currentCard, CardMarker.FLAG_ORANGE)
 430→            }
 431→            R.id.action_flag_three -> {
 432→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Flag three")
 433→                onFlag(currentCard, CardMarker.FLAG_GREEN)
 434→            }
 435→            R.id.action_flag_four -> {
 436→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Flag four")
 437→                onFlag(currentCard, CardMarker.FLAG_BLUE)
 438→            }
 439→            R.id.action_flag_five -> {
 440→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Flag five")
 441→                onFlag(currentCard, CardMarker.FLAG_PINK)
 442→            }
 443→            R.id.action_flag_six -> {
 444→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Flag six")
 445→                onFlag(currentCard, CardMarker.FLAG_TURQUOISE)
 446→            }
 447→            R.id.action_flag_seven -> {
 448→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Flag seven")
 449→                onFlag(currentCard, CardMarker.FLAG_PURPLE)
 450→            }
 451→            R.id.action_card_info -> {
 452→                Timber.i("Card Viewer:: Card Info")
 453→                openCardInfo()
 454→            }
 455→            else -> {
 456→                return super.onOptionsItemSelected(item)
 457→            }
 458→        }
 459→        return true
 460→    }
 461→
 462→    public override fun toggleWhiteboard() {
 463→        prefWhiteboard = !prefWhiteboard
 464→        Timber.i("Reviewer:: Whiteboard enabled state set to %b", prefWhiteboard)
 465→        // Even though the visibility is now stored in its own setting, we want it to be dependent
 466→        // on the enabled status
 467→        setWhiteboardEnabledState(prefWhiteboard)
 468→        setWhiteboardVisibility(prefWhiteboard)
 469→        if (!prefWhiteboard) {
 470→            mColorPalette.visibility = View.GONE
 471→        }
 472→        refreshActionBar()
 473→    }
 474→
 475→    public override fun clearWhiteboard() {
 476→        if (whiteboard != null) {
 477→            whiteboard!!.clear()
 478→        }
 479→    }
 480→
 481→    public override fun changeWhiteboardPenColor() {
 482→        if (mColorPalette.visibility == View.GONE) {
 483→            mColorPalette.visibility = View.VISIBLE
 484→        } else {
 485→            mColorPalette.visibility = View.GONE
 486→        }
 487→        updateWhiteboardEditorPosition()
 488→    }
 489→
 490→    override fun replayVoice() {
 491→        if (!openMicToolbar()) {
 492→            return
 493→        }
 494→
 495→        // COULD_BE_BETTER: this shows "Failed" if nothing was recorded
 496→        audioView!!.togglePlay()
 497→    }
 498→
 499→    override fun recordVoice() {
 500→        if (!openMicToolbar()) {
 501→            return
 502→        }
 503→        audioView!!.toggleRecord()
 504→    }
 505→
 506→    override fun updateForNewCard() {
 507→        super.updateForNewCard()
 508→        if (prefWhiteboard && whiteboard != null) {
 509→            whiteboard!!.clear()
 510→        }
 511→    }
 512→
 513→    override fun unblockControls() {
 514→        if (prefWhiteboard && whiteboard != null) {
 515→            whiteboard!!.isEnabled = true
 516→        }
 517→        super.unblockControls()
 518→    }
 519→
 520→    public override fun blockControls(quick: Boolean) {
 521→        if (prefWhiteboard && whiteboard != null) {
 522→            whiteboard!!.isEnabled = false
 523→        }
 524→        super.blockControls(quick)
 525→    }
 526→
 527→    override fun closeReviewer(result: Int) {
 528→        // Stop the mic recording if still pending
 529→        audioView?.notifyStopRecord()
 530→
 531→        // Remove the temporary audio file
 532→        tempAudioPath?.let {
 533→            val tempAudioPathToDelete = File(it)
 534→            if (tempAudioPathToDelete.exists()) {
 535→                tempAudioPathToDelete.delete()
 536→            }
 537→        }
 538→        super.closeReviewer(result)
 539→    }
 540→
 541→    /**
 542→     *
 543→     * @return Whether the mic toolbar is usable
 544→     */
 545→    @VisibleForTesting
 546→    fun openMicToolbar(): Boolean {
 547→        if (audioView == null || audioView!!.visibility != View.VISIBLE) {
 548→            openOrToggleMicToolbar()
 549→        }
 550→        return audioView != null
 551→    }
 552→
 553→    private fun openOrToggleMicToolbar() {
 554→        if (!canRecordAudio(this)) {
 555→            ActivityCompat.requestPermissions(
 556→                this,
 557→                arrayOf(Manifest.permission.RECORD_AUDIO),
 558→                REQUEST_AUDIO_PERMISSION
 559→            )
 560→        } else {
 561→            toggleMicToolBar()
 562→        }
 563→    }
 564→
 565→    private fun toggleMicToolBar() {
 566→        audioView?.let {
 567→            it.visibility = if (it.visibility != View.VISIBLE) View.VISIBLE else View.GONE
 568→            return
 569→        }
 570→        // Record mic tool bar does not exist yet
 571→        tempAudioPath = generateTempAudioFile(this)
 572→        if (tempAudioPath == null) {
 573→            return
 574→        }
 575→        audioView = createRecorderInstance(
 576→            this,
 577→            R.drawable.ic_play_arrow_white_24dp,
 578→            R.drawable.ic_pause_white_24dp,
 579→            R.drawable.ic_stop_white_24dp,
 580→            R.drawable.ic_rec,
 581→            R.drawable.ic_rec_stop,
 582→            tempAudioPath!!
 583→        )
 584→        if (audioView == null) {
 585→            tempAudioPath = null
 586→            return
 587→        }
 588→        val lp2 = FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
 589→            ViewGroup.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
 590→            ViewGroup.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
 591→        )

 691→
 692→        // Undo button
 693→        @DrawableRes val undoIconId: Int

 936→    private fun updateScreenCounts() {
 937→        val queue = queueState ?: return
 938→        super.updateActionBar()
 939→        val actionBar = supportActionBar
 940→        val counts = queue.counts
 941→        if (actionBar != null) {
 942→            if (mPrefShowETA) {
 943→                launchCatchingTask {
 944→                    mEta = withCol { sched.eta(counts, false) }
 945→                    actionBar.subtitle = remainingTime(this@Reviewer, (mEta * 60).toLong())
 946→                }
 947→            }
 948→        }
 949→        mNewCount = SpannableString(counts.new.toString())
 950→        mLrnCount = SpannableString(counts.lrn.toString())
 951→        mRevCount = SpannableString(counts.rev.toString())
 952→        if (mPrefHideDueCount) {
 953→            mRevCount = SpannableString("???")
 954→        }
 955→        // if this code is run as a card is being answered, currentCard may be non-null but
 956→        // the queues may be empty - we can't call countIdx() in such a case
 957→        if (counts.count() != 0) {
 958→            when (queue.countsIndex) {
 959→                Counts.Queue.NEW -> mNewCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mNewCount!!.length, 0)
 960→                Counts.Queue.LRN -> mLrnCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mLrnCount!!.length, 0)
 961→                Counts.Queue.REV -> mRevCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mRevCount!!.length, 0)
 962→            }
 963→        }
 964→        mTextBarNew.text = mNewCount
 965→        mTextBarLearn.text = mLrnCount
 966→        mTextBarReview.text = mRevCount
 967→    }

 996→    override suspend fun answerCardInner(ease: Int) {
 997→        val state = queueState!!
 998→        Timber.d("answerCardInner: ${currentCard!!.id} $ease")
 999→        var wasLeech = false
1000→        undoableOp(this) {
1001→            sched.answerCard(state, ease).also {
1002→                wasLeech = sched.againIsLeech(state)
1003→            }
1004→        }.also {
1005→            if (ease == Consts.BUTTON_ONE && wasLeech) {
1006→                state.topCard.load()
1007→                val leechMessage: String = if (state.topCard.queue < 0) {
1008→                    resources.getString(R.string.leech_suspend_notification)
1009→                } else {
1010→                    resources.getString(R.string.leech_notification)
1011→                }
1012→                showSnackbar(leechMessage, Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT)
1013→            }
1014→        }
1015→    }

1017→    private fun dealWithTimeBox(timebox: Collection.TimeboxReached) {
1018→        val nCards = timebox.reps
1019→        val nMins = timebox.secs / 60
1020→        val mins = resources.getQuantityString(R.plurals.in_minutes, nMins, nMins)
1021→        val timeboxMessage = resources.getQuantityString(R.plurals.timebox_reached, nCards, nCards, mins)
1022→        AlertDialog.Builder(this).show {
1023→            title(R.string.timebox_reached_title)
1024→            message(text = timeboxMessage)
1025→            positiveButton(R.string.dialog_continue) {}
1026→            negativeButton(text = CollectionManager.TR.studyingFinish()) {
1027→                finishWithAnimation(ActivityTransitionAnimation.Direction.END)
1028→            }
1029→            cancelable(true)
1030→            setOnCancelListener { }
1031→        }
1032→    }

1047→    private fun runStateMutationHook() {
1048→        val state = queueState ?: return
1049→        if (state.customSchedulingJs.isEmpty()) {
1050→            return
1051→        }
1052→        val key = customSchedulingKey
1053→        val js = state.customSchedulingJs
1054→        webView?.evaluateJavascript(
1055→            """
1056→        anki.mutateNextCardStates('$key', async (states, customData, ctx) => {{ $js }})
1057→            .catch(err => console.log(err));
1058→"""
1059→        ) {}
1060→    }

1105→    override fun executeCommand(which: ViewerCommand, fromGesture: Gesture?): Boolean {
1106→        if (isControlBlocked && which !== ViewerCommand.EXIT) {
1107→            return false
1108→        }
1109→        when (which) {
1110→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> {
1111→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_RED)
1112→                return true
1113→            }
1114→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> {
1115→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_ORANGE)
1116→                return true
1117→            }
1118→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> {
1119→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_GREEN)
1120→                return true
1121→            }
1122→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> {
1123→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_BLUE)
1124→                return true
1125→            }
1126→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> {
1127→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_PINK)
1128→                return true
1129→            }
1130→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> {
1131→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_TURQUOISE)
1132→                return true
1133→            }
1134→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> {
1135→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_PURPLE)
1136→                return true
1137→            }
1138→            ViewerCommand.UNSET_FLAG -> {
1139→                onFlag(currentCard, CardMarker.FLAG_NONE)
1140→                return true
1141→            }
1142→            ViewerCommand.MARK -> {
1143→                onMark(currentCard)
1144→                return true
1145→            }
1146→            ViewerCommand.ADD_NOTE -> {
1147→                addNote(fromGesture)
1148→                return true
1149→            }
1150→            ViewerCommand.CARD_INFO -> {
1151→                openCardInfo(fromGesture)
1152→                return true
1153→            }
1154→            ViewerCommand.RESCHEDULE_NOTE -> {
1155→                showRescheduleCardDialog()
1156→                return true
1157→            }
1158→            else -> return super.executeCommand(which, fromGesture)
1159→        }
1160→    }

1226→    @Suppress("deprecation") // #9332: UI Visibility -> Insets
1227→    private fun setFullScreen(a: AbstractFlashcardViewer) {
1228→        // Set appropriate flags to enable Sticky Immersive mode.
1229→        a.window.decorView.systemUiVisibility = (
1230→            View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_STABLE // | View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_HIDE_NAVIGATION // temporarily disabled due to #5245
1231→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_FULLSCREEN
1232→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_HIDE_NAVIGATION
1233→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_FULLSCREEN
1234→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LOW_PROFILE
1235→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_IMMERSIVE
1236→            )
1237→        // Show / hide the Action bar together with the status bar
1238→        val prefs = a.sharedPrefs()
1239→        val fullscreenMode = fromPreference(prefs)
1240→        a.window.statusBarColor = MaterialColors.getColor(a, R.attr.appBarColor, 0)
1241→        val decorView = a.window.decorView
1242→        decorView.setOnSystemUiVisibilityChangeListener { flags: Int ->
1243→            val toolbar = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.toolbar)
1244→            val answerButtons = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.answer_options_layout)
1245→            val topbar = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.top_bar)
1246→            if (toolbar == null || topbar == null || answerButtons == null) {
1247→                return@setOnSystemUiVisibilityChangeListener
1248→            }
1249→            // Note that system bars will only be "visible" if none of the
1250→            // LOW_PROFILE, HIDE_NAVIGATION, or FULLSCREEN flags are set.
1251→            val visible = flags and View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_HIDE_NAVIGATION == 0
1252→            Timber.d("System UI visibility change. Visible: %b", visible)
1253→            if (visible) {
1254→                showViewWithAnimation(toolbar)
1255→                if (fullscreenMode == FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE) {
1256→                    showViewWithAnimation(topbar)
1257→                    showViewWithAnimation(answerButtons)
1258→                }
1259→            } else {
1260→                hideViewWithAnimation(toolbar)
1261→                if (fullscreenMode == FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE) {
1262→                    hideViewWithAnimation(topbar)
1263→                    hideViewWithAnimation(answerButtons)
1264→                }
1265→            }
1266→        }
1267→    }
1268→
1269→    private fun showViewWithAnimation(view: View) {
1270→        view.alpha = 0.0f
1271→        view.visibility = View.VISIBLE
1272→        view.animate().alpha(TRANSPARENCY).setDuration(ANIMATION_DURATION.toLong()).setListener(null)
1273→    }
1274→
1275→    private fun hideViewWithAnimation(view: View) {
1276→        view.animate()
1277→            .alpha(0f)
1278→            .setDuration(ANIMATION_DURATION.toLong())
1279→            .setListener(object : AnimatorListenerAdapter() {
1280→                override fun onAnimationEnd(animation: Animator) {
1281→                    view.visibility = View.GONE
1282→                }
1283→            })
1284→    }

1291→    private fun createWhiteboard() {
1292→        whiteboard = createInstance(this, true, this)
1293→
1294→        // We use the pen color of the selected deck at the time the whiteboard is enabled.
1295→        // This is how all other whiteboard settings are
1296→        val whiteboardPenColor = MetaDB.getWhiteboardPenColor(this, parentDid).fromPreferences()
1297→        if (whiteboardPenColor != null) {
1298→            whiteboard!!.penColor = whiteboardPenColor
1299→        }
1300→        whiteboard!!.setOnPaintColorChangeListener(object : OnPaintColorChangeListener {
1301→            override fun onPaintColorChange(color: Int?) {
1302→                MetaDB.storeWhiteboardPenColor(this@Reviewer, parentDid, !currentTheme.isNightMode, color)
1303→            }
1304→        })
1305→        whiteboard!!.setOnTouchListener { v: View, event: MotionEvent? ->
1306→            if (event == null) return@setOnTouchListener false
1307→            // If the whiteboard is currently drawing, and triggers the system UI to show, we want to continue drawing.
1308→            if (!whiteboard!!.isCurrentlyDrawing && (
1309→                !mShowWhiteboard || (
1310→                    mPrefFullscreenReview &&
1311→                        isImmersiveSystemUiVisible(this@Reviewer)
1312→                    )
1313→                )
1314→            ) {
1315→                // Bypass whiteboard listener when it's hidden or fullscreen immersive mode is temporarily suspended
1316→                v.performClick()
1317→                return@setOnTouchListener gestureDetector!!.onTouchEvent(event)
1318→            }
1319→            whiteboard!!.handleTouchEvent(event)
1320→        }
1321→    }

1367→            getColUnsafe.db.queryScalar(
1368→                "select 1 from cards where nid = ? and id != ? and queue != " + Consts.QUEUE_TYPE_SUSPENDED + " limit 1",
1369→                currentCard!!.nid,
1370→                currentCard!!.id

1372→        }

17→package com.ichi2.anki

19→import androidx.fragment.app.FragmentActivity
20→import anki.collection.OpChangesAfterUndo
21→import com.google.android.material.snackbar.Snackbar
22→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.TR
23→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
24→import com.ichi2.libanki.undo
25→import com.ichi2.libanki.undoableOp
26→
27→/** If there's an action pending in the review queue, undo it and show a snackbar */
28→suspend fun FragmentActivity.undoAndShowSnackbar(duration: Int = Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT) {
29→    withProgress {
30→        val changes = undoableOp {
31→            if (!undoAvailable()) {
32→                OpChangesAfterUndo.getDefaultInstance()
33→            } else {
34→                undo()
35→            }
36→        }
37→        val message = if (changes.operation.isEmpty()) {
38→            TR.actionsNothingToUndo()
39→        } else {
40→            TR.undoActionUndone(changes.operation)
41→        }
42→        showSnackbar(message, duration)
43→    }
44→}

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.reviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 20→import android.view.Menu
 21→import android.view.MenuItem
 22→import androidx.annotation.IdRes
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 24→import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
 25→import com.ichi2.utils.HashUtil.hashMapInit
 26→
 27→// loads of unboxing issues, which are safe
 28→class ActionButtonStatus(private val reviewerUi: ReviewerUi) {
 29→    /**
 30→     * Custom button allocation
 31→     */
 32→    private val mCustomButtons: MutableMap<Int, Int> = hashMapInit(25) // setup's size
 33→
 34→    fun setup(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
 35→        // NOTE: the default values below should be in sync with preferences_custom_buttons.xml and reviewer.xml
 36→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_undo, "customButtonUndo", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 37→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_schedule, "customButtonScheduleCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 38→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_flag, "customButtonFlag", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 39→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_tag, "customButtonTags", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 40→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_edit, "customButtonEditCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 41→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_add_note_reviewer, "customButtonAddCard", MENU_DISABLED)
 42→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_replay, "customButtonReplay", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 43→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_card_info, "customButtonCardInfo", MENU_DISABLED)
 44→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_clear_whiteboard, "customButtonClearWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 45→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_hide_whiteboard, "customButtonShowHideWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 46→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_select_tts, "customButtonSelectTts", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 47→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_open_deck_options, "customButtonDeckOptions", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 48→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_bury, "customButtonBury", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 49→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_suspend, "customButtonSuspend", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 50→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_mark_card, "customButtonMarkCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 51→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_delete, "customButtonDelete", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 52→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_mic_tool_bar, "customButtonToggleMicToolBar", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 53→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_whiteboard, "customButtonEnableWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 54→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_stylus, "customButtonToggleStylus", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 55→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_save_whiteboard, "customButtonSaveWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 56→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color, "customButtonWhiteboardPenColor", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    private fun setupButton(preferences: SharedPreferences, @IdRes resourceId: Int, preferenceName: String, showAsActionType: Int) {
 60→        mCustomButtons[resourceId] = preferences.getString(
 61→            preferenceName,
 62→            showAsActionType.toString()
 63→        )!!.toInt()
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    fun setCustomButtons(menu: Menu) {
 67→        for ((itemId, value) in mCustomButtons) {
 68→            if (value != MENU_DISABLED) {
 69→                val item = menu.findItem(itemId)
 70→                item.setShowAsAction(value)
 71→                val icon = item.icon
 72→                item.isEnabled = !reviewerUi.isControlBlocked
 73→                if (icon != null) {
 74→                    /* Ideally, we want to give feedback to users that
 75→                    buttons are disabled.  However, some actions are
 76→                    expected to be so quick that the visual feedback
 77→                    is useless and is only seen as a flickering.
 78→
 79→                    We use a heuristic to decide whether the next card
 80→                    will appear quickly or slowly.  We change the
 81→                    color only if the buttons are blocked and we
 82→                    expect the next card to take time to arrive.
 83→                    */
 84→                    val mutableIcon = icon.mutate()
 85→                    if (reviewerUi.controlBlocked == ReviewerUi.ControlBlock.SLOW) {
 86→                        mutableIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 87→                    } else {
 88→                        mutableIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 89→                    }
 90→                }
 91→            } else {
 92→                menu.findItem(itemId).isVisible = false
 93→            }
 94→        }
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    fun hideWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 98→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_hide_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
 99→    }
100→
101→    fun toggleStylusIsDisabled(): Boolean {
102→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_toggle_stylus] == MENU_DISABLED
103→    }
104→
105→    fun clearWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
106→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_clear_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
107→    }
108→
109→    fun selectTtsIsDisabled(): Boolean {
110→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_select_tts] == MENU_DISABLED
111→    }
112→
113→    fun saveWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
114→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_save_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
115→    }
116→
117→    fun whiteboardPenColorIsDisabled(): Boolean {
118→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color] == MENU_DISABLED
119→    }
120→
121→    companion object {
122→        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER
123→        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM
124→        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS
125→        const val MENU_DISABLED = 3
126→    }
127→}
128→

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 17→
 18→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 19→import android.view.KeyEvent
 20→import androidx.core.content.edit
 21→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.Companion.keyCode
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.Companion.unicode
 24→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.ModifierKeys
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.ModifierKeys.Companion.ctrl
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.ModifierKeys.Companion.shift
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.CardSide
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.fromPreference
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
 31→import java.util.*
 32→import java.util.function.BiFunction
 33→import java.util.stream.Collectors
 34→
 35→/** Abstraction: Discuss moving many of these to 'Reviewer'  */
 36→enum class ViewerCommand(val resourceId: Int) {
 37→    SHOW_ANSWER(R.string.show_answer),
 38→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1(R.string.answer_again),
 39→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2(R.string.answer_hard),
 40→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3(R.string.answer_good),
 41→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4(R.string.answer_easy),
 42→    UNDO(R.string.undo),
 43→    EDIT(R.string.cardeditor_title_edit_card),
 44→    MARK(R.string.menu_mark_note),
 45→    BURY_CARD(R.string.menu_bury_card),
 46→    SUSPEND_CARD(R.string.menu_suspend_card),
 47→    DELETE(R.string.menu_delete_note),
 48→    PLAY_MEDIA(R.string.gesture_play),
 49→    EXIT(R.string.gesture_abort_learning),
 50→    BURY_NOTE(R.string.menu_bury_note),
 51→    SUSPEND_NOTE(R.string.menu_suspend_note),
 52→    TOGGLE_FLAG_RED(R.string.gesture_flag_red),
 53→    TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE(R.string.gesture_flag_orange),
 54→    TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN(R.string.gesture_flag_green),
 55→    TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE(R.string.gesture_flag_blue),
 56→    TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK(R.string.gesture_flag_pink),
 57→    TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE(R.string.gesture_flag_turquoise),
 58→    TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE(R.string.gesture_flag_purple),
 59→    UNSET_FLAG(R.string.gesture_flag_remove),
 60→    PAGE_UP(R.string.gesture_page_up),
 61→    PAGE_DOWN(R.string.gesture_page_down),
 62→    TAG(R.string.add_tag),
 63→    CARD_INFO(R.string.card_info_title),
 64→    ABORT_AND_SYNC(R.string.gesture_abort_sync),
 65→    RECORD_VOICE(R.string.record_voice),
 66→    REPLAY_VOICE(R.string.replay_voice),
 67→    TOGGLE_WHITEBOARD(R.string.gesture_toggle_whiteboard),
 68→    CLEAR_WHITEBOARD(R.string.clear_whiteboard),
 69→    CHANGE_WHITEBOARD_PEN_COLOR(R.string.title_whiteboard_editor),
 70→    SHOW_HINT(R.string.gesture_show_hint),
 71→    SHOW_ALL_HINTS(R.string.gesture_show_all_hints),
 72→    ADD_NOTE(R.string.menu_add_note),
 73→    RESCHEDULE_NOTE(R.string.card_editor_reschedule_card);
 74→
 75→    companion object {
 76→        val allDefaultBindings: List<MappableBinding>
 77→            get() = Arrays.stream(values())
 78→                .flatMap { x: ViewerCommand -> x.defaultValue.stream() }
 79→                .collect(Collectors.toList())
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    val preferenceKey: String
 83→        get() = "binding_$name"
 84→
 85→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 86→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 87→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 88→            collection.remove(element)
 89→            collection.add(0, element)
 90→            true
 91→        }
 92→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 96→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 97→            // do not reorder the elements
 98→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
 99→                return@BiFunction false
100→            }
101→            collection.add(element)
102→            return@BiFunction true
103→        }
104→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
108→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
109→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
110→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
111→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
112→    }
113→
114→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
115→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
116→        bindings.remove(binding)
117→        prefs.edit {
118→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
123→    val defaultValue: List<MappableBinding>
124→        get() = // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
125→            when (this) {
126→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1 -> from(
127→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_Y, CardSide.BOTH),
128→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.ANSWER),
129→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.ANSWER)
130→                )
131→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2 -> from(
132→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_X, CardSide.BOTH),
133→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.ANSWER),
134→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.ANSWER)
135→                )
136→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3 -> from(
137→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_B, CardSide.BOTH),
138→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
139→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
140→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
141→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
142→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
143→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
144→                )
145→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> from(
146→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
147→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
148→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
149→                )
150→                EDIT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
151→                MARK -> from(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
152→                BURY_CARD -> from(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
153→                BURY_NOTE -> from(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
154→                SUSPEND_CARD -> from(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
155→                SUSPEND_NOTE -> from(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
156→                PLAY_MEDIA -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
157→                REPLAY_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
158→                RECORD_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
159→                UNDO -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
160→                TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
161→                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
162→                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
163→                TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
164→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
165→                TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
166→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
167→                SHOW_HINT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_H, CardSide.BOTH))
168→                SHOW_ALL_HINTS -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_G, CardSide.BOTH))
169→                ADD_NOTE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_A, CardSide.BOTH))
170→                else -> ArrayList()
171→            }
172→
173→    private fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, keys: ModifierKeys): MappableBinding {
174→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
175→    }
176→
177→    private fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
178→        return MappableBinding(unicode(c), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun from(vararg bindings: MappableBinding): List<MappableBinding> {
182→        return ArrayList(listOf(*bindings))
183→    }
184→
185→    private fun keyCode(keyCode: Int, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
186→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keyCode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
187→    }
188→
189→    fun interface CommandProcessor {
190→        /**
191→         *
192→         * example failure: answering an ease on the front of the card
193→         */
194→        fun executeCommand(which: ViewerCommand, fromGesture: Gesture?): Boolean
195→    }
196→}
197→

1→/*
  2→ *  Copyright (c) 2021 David Allison <davidallisongithub@gmail.com>
  3→ *

  5→ *  the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software
  6→ *  Foundation; either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later
  7→ *  version.
  8→ *
  9→ *  This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY
 10→ *  WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A
 11→ *  PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.
 12→ *
 13→ *  You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with
 14→ *  this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 15→ */
 16→package com.ichi2.anki.reviewer
 17→
 18→import android.content.Context
 19→import android.view.KeyEvent
 20→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 21→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.Gesture
 22→import com.ichi2.utils.StringUtil
 23→import timber.log.Timber
 24→
 25→class Binding private constructor(val modifierKeys: ModifierKeys?, val keycode: Int?, val unicodeCharacter: Char?, val gesture: Gesture?) {
 26→    constructor(gesture: Gesture?) : this(null, null, null, gesture)
 27→
 28→    private fun getKeyCodePrefix(): String {
 29→        val keyPrefix = KEY_PREFIX.toString()
 30→
 31→        if (keycode == null) {
 32→            return keyPrefix
 33→        }
 34→
 35→        if (KeyEvent.isGamepadButton(keycode)) {
 36→            return GAMEPAD_PREFIX
 37→        }
 38→
 39→        return keyPrefix
 40→    }
 41→    fun toDisplayString(context: Context?): String {
 42→        val string = StringBuilder()
 43→        when {
 44→            keycode != null -> {
 45→                string.append(getKeyCodePrefix())
 46→                string.append(' ')
 47→                string.append(modifierKeys!!.toString())
 48→                val keyCodeString = KeyEvent.keyCodeToString(keycode)
 49→                // replace "Button" as we use the gamepad icon
 50→                string.append(StringUtil.toTitleCase(keyCodeString.replace("KEYCODE_", "").replace("BUTTON_", "").replace('_', ' ')))
 51→            }
 52→            unicodeCharacter != null -> {
 53→                string.append(KEY_PREFIX)
 54→                string.append(' ')
 55→                string.append(modifierKeys!!.toString())
 56→                string.append(unicodeCharacter)
 57→            }
 58→            gesture != null -> {
 59→                string.append(gesture.toDisplayString(context!!))
 60→            }
 61→        }
 62→        return string.toString()
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    override fun toString(): String {
 66→        val string = StringBuilder()
 67→        when {
 68→            keycode != null -> {
 69→                string.append(KEY_PREFIX)
 70→                string.append(modifierKeys!!.toString())
 71→                string.append(keycode)
 72→            }
 73→            unicodeCharacter != null -> {
 74→                string.append(UNICODE_PREFIX)
 75→                string.append(modifierKeys!!.toString())
 76→                string.append(unicodeCharacter)
 77→            }
 78→            gesture != null -> {
 79→                string.append(GESTURE_PREFIX)
 80→                string.append(gesture)
 81→            }
 82→        }
 83→        return string.toString()
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    val isValid: Boolean get() = isKey || gesture != null
 87→    val isKeyCode: Boolean get() = keycode != null
 88→
 89→    val isKey: Boolean
 90→        get() = isKeyCode || unicodeCharacter != null
 91→
 92→    val isGesture: Boolean = gesture != null
 93→
 94→    open class ModifierKeys internal constructor(private val shift: Boolean, private val ctrl: Boolean, private val alt: Boolean) {
 95→        fun matches(event: KeyEvent): Boolean {
 96→            // return false if Ctrl+1 is pressed and 1 is expected
 97→            return shiftMatches(event) && ctrlMatches(event) && altMatches(event)
 98→        }
 99→
100→        private fun shiftMatches(event: KeyEvent): Boolean = shift == event.isShiftPressed
101→
102→        private fun ctrlMatches(event: KeyEvent): Boolean = ctrl == event.isCtrlPressed
103→
104→        private fun altMatches(event: KeyEvent): Boolean = altMatches(event.isAltPressed)
105→
106→        open fun shiftMatches(shiftPressed: Boolean): Boolean = shift == shiftPressed
107→
108→        fun ctrlMatches(ctrlPressed: Boolean): Boolean = ctrl == ctrlPressed
109→
110→        fun altMatches(altPressed: Boolean): Boolean = alt == altPressed
111→
112→        override fun toString(): String {
113→            val string = StringBuilder()
114→            if (ctrl) {
115→                string.append("Ctrl+")
116→            }
117→            if (alt) {
118→                string.append("Alt+")
119→            }
120→            if (shift) {
121→                string.append("Shift+")
122→            }
123→            return string.toString()
124→        }
125→
126→        companion object {
127→            fun none(): ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys(shift = false, ctrl = false, alt = false)
128→
129→            fun ctrl(): ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys(shift = false, ctrl = true, alt = false)
130→
131→            fun shift(): ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys(shift = true, ctrl = false, alt = false)
132→
133→            fun alt(): ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys(shift = false, ctrl = false, alt = true)
134→
135→            /**
136→             * Parses a [ModifierKeys] from a string.
137→             * @param s The string to parse
138→             * @return The [ModifierKeys], and the remainder of the string
139→             */
140→            fun parse(s: String): Pair<ModifierKeys, String> {
141→                var modifiers = none()
142→                val plus = s.lastIndexOf("+")
143→                if (plus == -1) {
144→                    return Pair(modifiers, s)
145→                }
146→                modifiers = fromString(s.substring(0, plus + 1))
147→                return Pair(modifiers, s.substring(plus + 1))
148→            }
149→
150→            fun fromString(from: String): ModifierKeys =
151→                ModifierKeys(from.contains("Shift"), from.contains("Ctrl"), from.contains("Alt"))
152→        }
153→    }
154→
155→    /** Modifier keys which cannot be defined by a binding  */
156→    private class AppDefinedModifierKeys private constructor() : ModifierKeys(false, false, false) {
157→        override fun shiftMatches(shiftPressed: Boolean): Boolean = true
158→
159→        companion object {
160→            /**
161→             * Specifies a keycode combination binding from an unknown input device
162→             * Should be due to the "default" key bindings and never from user input
163→             *
164→             * If we do not know what the device is, "*" could be a key on the keyboard or Shift + 8
165→             *
166→             * So we need to ignore shift, rather than match it to a value
167→             *
168→             * If we have bindings in the app, then we know whether we need shift or not (in actual fact, we should
169→             * be fine to use keycodes).
170→             */
171→            fun allowShift(): ModifierKeys = AppDefinedModifierKeys()
172→        }
173→    }
174→
175→    companion object {
176→        const val FORBIDDEN_UNICODE_CHAR = MappableBinding.PREF_SEPARATOR
177→
178→        /**
179→         * https://www.fileformat.info/info/unicode/char/2328/index.htm (Keyboard)
180→         * This is not usable on API 21 or 22
181→         */
182→        const val KEY_PREFIX = '\u2328'
183→
184→        /** https://www.fileformat.info/info/unicode/char/235d/index.htm (similar to a finger)  */
185→        const val GESTURE_PREFIX = '\u235D'
186→
187→        /** https://www.fileformat.info/info/unicode/char/2705/index.htm - checkmark (often used in URLs for unicode)
188→         * Only used for serialisation. [.KEY_PREFIX] is used for display.
189→         */
190→        const val UNICODE_PREFIX = '\u2705'
191→
192→        const val GAMEPAD_PREFIX = "🎮"
193→
194→        /** This returns multiple bindings due to the "default" implementation not knowing what the keycode for a button is  */
195→        fun key(event: KeyEvent): List<Binding> {
196→            val modifiers = ModifierKeys(event.isShiftPressed, event.isCtrlPressed, event.isAltPressed)
197→            val ret: MutableList<Binding> = ArrayList()
198→            val keyCode = event.keyCode
199→            if (keyCode != 0) {
200→                ret.add(keyCode(modifiers, keyCode))
201→            }
202→
203→            // passing in metaState: 0 means that Ctrl+1 returns '1' instead of '\0'
204→            // NOTE: We do not differentiate on upper/lower case via KeyEvent.META_CAPS_LOCK_ON
205→            val unicodeChar = event.getUnicodeChar(event.metaState and (KeyEvent.META_SHIFT_ON or KeyEvent.META_NUM_LOCK_ON))
206→            if (unicodeChar != 0) {
207→                try {
208→                    ret.add(unicode(modifiers, unicodeChar.toChar()))
209→                } catch (e: Exception) {
210→                    Timber.w(e)
211→                }
212→            }
213→
214→            return ret
215→        }
216→
217→        /**
218→         * Specifies a unicode binding from an unknown input device
219→         * See [AppDefinedModifierKeys]
220→         */
221→        fun unicode(unicodeChar: Char): Binding =
222→            unicode(AppDefinedModifierKeys.allowShift(), unicodeChar)
223→
224→        fun unicode(modifierKeys: ModifierKeys?, unicodeChar: Char): Binding {
225→            if (unicodeChar == FORBIDDEN_UNICODE_CHAR) return unknown()
226→            return Binding(modifierKeys, null, unicodeChar, null)
227→        }
228→
229→        fun keyCode(keyCode: Int): Binding = keyCode(ModifierKeys.none(), keyCode)
230→
231→        fun keyCode(modifiers: ModifierKeys?, keyCode: Int): Binding =
232→            Binding(modifiers, keyCode, null, null)
233→
234→        fun gesture(gesture: Gesture?): Binding = Binding(null, null, null, gesture)
235→
236→        @VisibleForTesting
237→        fun unknown(): Binding = Binding(ModifierKeys.none(), null, null, null)
238→
239→        fun fromString(from: String): Binding {
240→            if (from.isEmpty()) return unknown()
241→            try {
242→                return when (from[0]) {
243→                    GESTURE_PREFIX -> {
244→                        gesture(Gesture.valueOf(from.substring(1)))
245→                    }
246→                    UNICODE_PREFIX -> {
247→                        val parsed = ModifierKeys.parse(from.substring(1))
248→                        unicode(parsed.first, parsed.second[0])
249→                    }
250→                    KEY_PREFIX -> {
251→                        val parsed = ModifierKeys.parse(from.substring(1))
252→                        val keyCode = parsed.second.toInt()
253→                        keyCode(parsed.first, keyCode)
254→                    }
255→                    else -> unknown()
256→                }
257→            } catch (ex: Exception) {
258→                Timber.w(ex)
259→            }
260→            return unknown()
261→        }
262→    }
263→}

33→class DecksTest : JvmTest() {
34→    @Test
35→    fun test_remove() {
36→        val col = col
37→        // create a new col, and add a note/card to it
38→        val deck1 = addDeck("deck1")
39→        val note = col.newNote()
40→        note.setItem("Front", "1")
41→        note.notetype.put("did", deck1)
42→        col.addNote(note)
43→        val c = note.cards()[0]
44→        assertEquals(deck1, c.did)
45→        assertEquals(1, col.cardCount().toLong())
46→        col.decks.removeDecks(listOf(deck1))
47→        assertEquals(0, col.cardCount().toLong())
48→        // if we try to get it, we get the default
49→        assertEquals("[no deck]", col.decks.name(c.did))
50→    }
51→
52→    @Test
53→    @SuppressLint("CheckResult")
54→    @Throws(DeckRenameException::class)
55→    fun test_rename() {
56→        val col = col
57→        var id = addDeck("hello::world")
58→        // should be able to rename into a completely different branch, creating
59→        // parents as necessary
60→        val decks = col.decks
61→        decks.rename(decks.get(id)!!, "foo::bar")
62→        var names: List<String> = decks.allNamesAndIds().map { it.name }
63→        assertTrue(names.contains("foo"))
64→        assertTrue(names.contains("foo::bar"))
65→        assertFalse(names.contains("hello::world"))
66→        // create another col
67→        /* TODO: do we want to follow upstream here ?
68→         // automatically adjusted if a duplicate name
69→         decks.rename(decks.get(id), "FOO");
70→         names =  decks.allSortedNames();
71→         assertThat(names, containsString("FOO+"));
72→
73→          */
74→        // when renaming, the children should be renamed too
75→        addDeck("one::two::three")
76→        id = addDeck("one")
77→        col.decks.rename(col.decks.get(id)!!, "yo")
78→        names = decks.allNamesAndIds().map { it.name }
79→        for (n in arrayOf("yo", "yo::two", "yo::two::three")) {
80→            assertTrue(names.contains(n))
81→        }
82→        // over filtered
83→        val filteredId = addDynamicDeck("filtered")
84→        col.decks.get(filteredId)
85→        val childId = addDeck("child")
86→        val child = col.decks.get(childId)!!
87→        assertThrows(DeckRenameException::class.java) {
88→            col.decks.rename(
89→                child,
90→                "filtered::child"
91→            )
92→        }
93→        assertThrows(DeckRenameException::class.java) {
94→            col.decks.rename(
95→                child,
96→                "FILTERED::child"
97→            )
98→        }
99→    }

 9→ PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.
10→ You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with
11→ this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
12→ */
13→package com.ichi2.ui
14→
15→import android.app.Activity
16→import android.content.Context
17→import android.content.ContextWrapper
18→import android.view.MenuItem
19→import android.view.View
20→import android.widget.ImageButton
21→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
22→import androidx.appcompat.widget.TooltipCompat
23→import com.ichi2.anki.ActionProviderCompat
24→import com.ichi2.anki.R
25→
26→/**
27→ * An Rtl version of a normal action view, where the drawable is mirrored
28→ */
29→class RtlCompliantActionProvider(context: Context) : ActionProviderCompat(context) {
30→    @VisibleForTesting
31→    val mActivity: Activity
32→
33→    /**
34→     * The action to perform when clicking the associated menu item. By default this delegates to
35→     * the activity's [Activity.onOptionsItemSelected] callback, but a custom action can be set and
36→     * used instead.
37→     */
38→    var clickHandler: (Activity, MenuItem) -> Unit = { activity, menuItem ->
39→        activity.onOptionsItemSelected(menuItem)
40→    }
41→
42→    override fun onCreateActionView(forItem: MenuItem): View {
43→        val actionView = ImageButton(context, null, android.R.attr.actionButtonStyle)
44→        TooltipCompat.setTooltipText(actionView, forItem.title)
45→        forItem.icon?.let {
46→            it.isAutoMirrored = true
47→            actionView.setImageDrawable(it)
48→        }
49→        actionView.id = R.id.action_undo
50→        actionView.setOnClickListener {
51→            if (!forItem.isEnabled) {
52→                return@setOnClickListener
53→            }
54→            clickHandler(mActivity, forItem)
55→        }
56→        return actionView
57→    }
58→
59→    companion object {
60→        /**
61→         * Unwrap a context to get the base activity back.
62→         * @param context a context that may be of type [ContextWrapper]
63→         * @return The activity of the passed context
64→         */
65→        private fun unwrapContext(context: Context): Activity {
66→            var unwrappedContext: Context? = context
67→            while (unwrappedContext !is Activity && unwrappedContext is ContextWrapper) {
68→                unwrappedContext = unwrappedContext.baseContext
69→            }
70→            return if (unwrappedContext is Activity) {
71→                unwrappedContext
72→            } else {
73→                throw ClassCastException("Passed context should be either an instanceof Activity or a ContextWrapper wrapping an Activity")
74→            }
75→        }
76→    }
77→
78→    init {
79→        mActivity = unwrapContext(context)
80→    }
81→}
82→
