 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.anki.analytics
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 21→import android.os.Build
 22→import android.webkit.WebSettings
 23→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 24→import androidx.core.content.edit
 25→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.GoogleAnalytics
 26→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.GoogleAnalyticsConfig
 27→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.httpclient.OkHttpClientImpl
 28→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.request.DefaultRequest
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.BuildConfig
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 33→import com.ichi2.utils.DisplayUtils
 34→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup
 35→import com.ichi2.utils.WebViewDebugging.hasSetDataDirectory
 36→import org.acra.ACRA
 37→import org.acra.util.Installation
 38→import timber.log.Timber
 39→
 40→@KotlinCleanup("see if we can make variables lazy, or properties without the `s` prefix")
 41→object UsageAnalytics {
 42→    const val ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY = "analyticsOptIn"
 43→
 44→    @KotlinCleanup("lateinit")
 45→    private var sAnalytics: GoogleAnalytics? = null
 46→    private var sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler: Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler? = null
 47→    private var sOptIn = false
 48→    private var sAnalyticsTrackingId: String? = null
 49→    private var sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = -1
 50→
 51→    /**
 52→     * Initialize the analytics provider - must be called prior to sending anything.
 53→     * Usage after that is static
 54→     * Note: may need to implement sampling strategy internally to limit hits, or not track Reviewer...
 55→     *
 56→     * @param context required to look up the analytics codes for the app
 57→     */
 58→    @Synchronized
 59→    fun initialize(context: Context): GoogleAnalytics? {
 60→        Timber.i("initialize()")
 61→        if (sAnalytics == null) {
 62→            Timber.d("App tracking id 'tid' = %s", getAnalyticsTag(context))
 63→            val gaConfig = GoogleAnalyticsConfig()
 64→                .setBatchingEnabled(true)
 65→                .setSamplePercentage(getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context))
 66→                .setBatchSize(1) // until this handles application termination we will lose hits if batch>1
 67→            sAnalytics = GoogleAnalytics.builder()
 68→                .withTrackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 69→                .withConfig(gaConfig)
 70→                .withDefaultRequest(
 71→                    AndroidDefaultRequest()
 72→                        .setAndroidRequestParameters(context)
 73→                        .applicationName(context.getString(R.string.app_name))
 74→                        .applicationVersion(BuildConfig.VERSION_CODE.toString())
 75→                        .applicationId(BuildConfig.APPLICATION_ID)
 76→                        .trackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 77→                        .clientId(Installation.id(context))
 78→                        .anonymizeIp(context.resources.getBoolean(R.bool.ga_anonymizeIp))
 79→                )
 80→                .withHttpClient(OkHttpClientImpl(gaConfig))
 81→                .build()
 82→        }
 83→        installDefaultExceptionHandler()
 84→        val userPrefs = context.sharedPrefs()
 85→        optIn = userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
 86→        userPrefs.registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener { sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String? ->
 87→            if (key == ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY) {
 88→                val newValue = sharedPreferences.getBoolean(key, false)
 89→                Timber.i("Setting analytics opt-in to: %b", newValue)
 90→                optIn = newValue
 91→            }
 92→        }
 93→        return sAnalytics
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    private fun getAnalyticsTag(context: Context): String? {
 97→        if (sAnalyticsTrackingId == null) {
 98→            sAnalyticsTrackingId = context.getString(R.string.ga_trackingId)
 99→        }
100→        return sAnalyticsTrackingId
101→    }
102→
103→    private fun getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context: Context): Int {
104→        if (sAnalyticsSamplePercentage == -1) {
105→            sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = context.resources.getInteger(R.integer.ga_sampleFrequency)
106→        }
107→        return sAnalyticsSamplePercentage
108→    }
109→
110→    fun setDevMode() {
111→        Timber.d("setDevMode() re-configuring for development analytics tagging")
112→        sAnalyticsTrackingId = "UA-125800786-2"
113→        sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = 100
114→        reInitialize()
115→    }
116→
117→    /**
118→     * We want to send an analytics hit on any exception, then chain to other handlers (e.g., ACRA)
119→     */
120→    @Synchronized
121→    private fun installDefaultExceptionHandler() {
122→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = Thread.getDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler()
123→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler { thread: Thread?, throwable: Throwable ->
124→            sendAnalyticsException(throwable, true)
125→            if (thread == null) {
126→                Timber.w("unexpected: thread was null")
127→                return@setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler
128→            }
129→            sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler!!.uncaughtException(thread, throwable)
130→        }
131→    }
132→
133→    /**
134→     * Reset the default exception handler
135→     */
136→    @Synchronized
137→    private fun unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler() {
138→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler)
139→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = null
140→    }
141→
142→    /**
143→     * Allow users to enable or disable analytics
144→     */
145→    @set:Synchronized
146→    private var optIn: Boolean
147→        get() {
148→            Timber.d("getOptIn() status: %s", sOptIn)
149→            return sOptIn
150→        }
151→        private set(optIn) {
152→            Timber.i("setOptIn(): from %s to %s", sOptIn, optIn)
153→            sOptIn = optIn
154→            sAnalytics!!.flush()
155→            sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled = optIn
156→            sAnalytics!!.performSamplingElection()
157→            Timber.d("setOptIn() optIn / sAnalytics.config().enabled(): %s/%s", sOptIn, sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled)
158→        }
159→
160→    /**
161→     * Set the analytics up to log things, goes to hit validator. Experimental.
162→     *
163→     * @param dryRun set to true if you want to log analytics hit but not dispatch
164→     */
165→    @Synchronized
166→    fun setDryRun(dryRun: Boolean) {
167→        Timber.i("setDryRun(): %s, warning dryRun is experimental", dryRun)
168→    }
169→
170→    /**
171→     * Re-Initialize the analytics provider
172→     */
173→    @Synchronized
174→    fun reInitialize() {
175→        // send any pending async hits, re-chain default exception handlers and re-init
176→        Timber.i("reInitialize()")
177→        sAnalytics!!.flush()
178→        sAnalytics = null
179→        unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler()
180→        initialize(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext)
181→    }
182→
183→    /**
184→     * Submit a screen for aggregation / analysis.
185→     * Intended for use to determine if / how features are being used
186→     *
187→     * @param object the result of Object.getClass().getSimpleName() will be used as the screen tag
188→     */
189→    @KotlinCleanup("rename object")
190→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`: Any) {
191→        sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`.javaClass.simpleName)
192→    }
193→
194→    /**
195→     * Submit a screen display with a synthetic name for aggregation / analysis
196→     * Intended for use if your class handles multiple screens you want to track separately
197→     *
198→     * @param screenName screenName the name to show in analysis reports
199→     */
200→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(screenName: String) {
201→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsScreenView(): %s", screenName)
202→        if (!optIn) {
203→            return
204→        }
205→        sAnalytics!!.screenView().screenName(screenName).sendAsync()
206→    }
207→
208→    /**
209→     * Send a detailed arbitrary analytics event, with noun/verb pairs and extra data if needed
210→     *
211→     * @param category the category of event, make your own but use a constant so reporting is good
212→     * @param action   the action the user performed
213→     * @param value    A value for the event, Integer.MIN_VALUE signifies caller shouldn't send the value
214→     * @param label    A label for the event, may be null
215→     */
216→    fun sendAnalyticsEvent(category: String, action: String, value: Int? = null, label: String? = null) {
217→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsEvent() category/action/value/label: %s/%s/%s/%s", category, action, value, label)
218→        if (!optIn) {
219→            return
220→        }
221→        val event = sAnalytics!!.event().eventCategory(category).eventAction(action)
222→        if (label != null) {
223→            event.eventLabel(label)
224→        }
225→        if (value != null) {
226→            event.eventValue(value)
227→        }
228→        event.sendAsync()
229→    }
230→
231→    /**
232→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis, parsed from the exception information
233→     *
234→     * @param t     Throwable to send for analysis
235→     * @param fatal whether it was fatal or not
236→     */
237→    fun sendAnalyticsException(t: Throwable, fatal: Boolean) {
238→        sendAnalyticsException(getCause(t).toString(), fatal)
239→    }
240→
241→    @KotlinCleanup("convert to sequence")
242→    fun getCause(t: Throwable): Throwable {
243→        var cause: Throwable?
244→        var result = t
245→        while (null != result.cause.also { cause = it } && result != cause) {
246→            result = cause!!
247→        }
248→        return result
249→    }
250→
251→    /**
252→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis
253→     *
254→     * @param description API limited to 100 characters, truncated here to 100 if needed
255→     * @param fatal       whether it was fatal or not
256→     */
257→    fun sendAnalyticsException(description: String, fatal: Boolean) {
258→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsException() description/fatal: %s/%s", description, fatal)
259→        if (!sOptIn) {
260→            return
261→        }
262→        sAnalytics!!.exception().exceptionDescription(description).exceptionFatal(fatal).sendAsync()
263→    }
264→
265→    internal fun canGetDefaultUserAgent(): Boolean {
266→        // #5502 - getDefaultUserAgent starts a WebView. We can't have two WebViews with the same data directory.
267→        // But ACRA starts an :acra process which does not terminate when AnkiDroid is restarted. https://crbug.com/558377
268→
269→        // if we're not under the ACRA process then we're fine to initialize a WebView
270→        return if (!ACRA.isACRASenderServiceProcess()) {
271→            true
272→        } else {
273→            hasSetDataDirectory()
274→        }
275→
276→        // If we have a custom data directory, then the crash will not occur.
277→    }
278→
279→    // A listener on this preference handles the rest
280→    var isEnabled: Boolean
281→        get() {
282→            val userPrefs = AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs()
283→            return userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
284→        }
285→        set(value) {
286→            // A listener on this preference handles the rest
287→            AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs().edit {
288→                putBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, value)
289→            }
290→        }
291→
292→    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
293→    fun resetForTests() {
294→        sAnalytics = null
295→    }
296→
297→    /**
298→     * An Android-specific device config generator. Without this it's "Desktop" and unknown for all hardware.
299→     * It is interesting to us what devices people use though (for instance: is Amazon Kindle support worth it?
300→     * Is anyone still using e-ink devices? How many people are on tablets? ChromeOS?)
301→     */
302→    private class AndroidDefaultRequest : DefaultRequest() {
303→        fun setAndroidRequestParameters(context: Context): DefaultRequest {
304→            // Are we running on really large screens or small screens? Send raw screen size
305→            try {
306→                val size = DisplayUtils.getDisplayDimensions(context)
307→                this.screenResolution(size.x.toString() + "x" + size.y)
308→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
309→                Timber.w(e)
310→                // nothing much to do here, it means we couldn't get WindowManager
311→            }
312→
313→            // We can have up to 20 of these - there might be other things we want to know
314→            // but simply seeing what hardware we are running on should be useful
315→            this.customDimension(1, Build.VERSION.RELEASE) // systemVersion, e.g. "7.1.1"  for Android 7.1.1
316→            this.customDimension(2, Build.BRAND) // brand e.g. "OnePlus"
317→            this.customDimension(3, Build.MODEL) // model e.g. "ONEPLUS A6013" for the 6T
318→            this.customDimension(4, Build.BOARD) // deviceId e.g. "sdm845" for the 6T
319→
320→            // This is important for google to auto-fingerprint us for default reporting
321→            // It is not possible to set operating system explicitly, there is no API or analytics parameter for it
322→            // Instead they respond that they auto-parse User-Agent strings for analytics attribution
323→            // For maximum analytics built-in report compatibility we will send the official WebView User-Agent string
324→            try {
325→                if (canGetDefaultUserAgent()) {
326→                    this.userAgent(WebSettings.getDefaultUserAgent(context))
327→                } else {
328→                    this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
329→                }
330→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
331→                Timber.w(e)
332→                // Catch RuntimeException as WebView initialization blows up in unpredictable ways
333→                // but analytics should never be a show-stopper
334→                this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
335→            }
336→            return this
337→        }
338→    }
339→
340→    object Category {
341→        const val SYNC = "Sync"
342→        const val LINK_CLICKED = "LinkClicked"
343→        const val SETTING = "Setting"
344→    }
345→
346→    /**
347→     * These Strings must not be changed as they are used for analytic comparisons between AnkiDroid versions.
348→     * If a new string is added here then the respective changes must also be made in AnalyticsConstantsTest.java
349→     * All the constant strings added here must be annotated with @AnalyticsConstant.
350→     */
351→    object Actions {
352→        /* Analytics actions used in Help Dialog*/
353→        @AnalyticsConstant
354→        val OPENED_HELPDIALOG = "Opened HelpDialogBox"
355→
356→        @AnalyticsConstant
357→        val OPENED_USING_ANKIDROID = "Opened Using AnkiDroid"
358→
359→        @AnalyticsConstant
360→        val OPENED_GET_HELP = "Opened Get Help"
361→
362→        @AnalyticsConstant
363→        val OPENED_SUPPORT_ANKIDROID = "Opened Support AnkiDroid"
364→
365→        @AnalyticsConstant
366→        val OPENED_COMMUNITY = "Opened Community"
367→
368→        @AnalyticsConstant
369→        val OPENED_PRIVACY = "Opened Privacy"
370→
371→        @AnalyticsConstant
372→        val OPENED_ANKIWEB_TERMS_AND_CONDITIONS = "Opened AnkiWeb Terms and Conditions"
373→
374→        @AnalyticsConstant
375→        val OPENED_ANKIDROID_PRIVACY_POLICY = "Opened AnkiDroid Privacy Policy"
376→
377→        @AnalyticsConstant
378→        val OPENED_ANKIWEB_PRIVACY_POLICY = "Opened AnkiWeb Privacy Policy"
379→
380→        @AnalyticsConstant
381→        val OPENED_ANKIDROID_MANUAL = "Opened AnkiDroid Manual"
382→
383→        @AnalyticsConstant
384→        val OPENED_ANKI_MANUAL = "Opened Anki Manual"
385→
386→        @AnalyticsConstant
387→        val OPENED_ANKIDROID_FAQ = "Opened AnkiDroid FAQ"
388→
389→        @AnalyticsConstant
390→        val OPENED_MAILING_LIST = "Opened Mailing List"
391→
392→        @AnalyticsConstant
393→        val OPENED_REPORT_BUG = "Opened Report a Bug"
394→
395→        @AnalyticsConstant
396→        val OPENED_DONATE = "Opened Donate"
397→

400→

403→

406→

409→

412→

445→    }
446→
447→    // TODO use some kind of constants instead of directly strings

1→/***************************************************************************************
  2→ * Copyright (c) 2022 Ankitects Pty Ltd <http://apps.ankiweb.net>                       *
  3→ *                                                                                      *
  4→ * This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under        *

  7→ * version.                                                                             *

 10→ * WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A      *
 11→ * PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.             *
 12→ *                                                                                      *
 13→ * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with         *
 14→ * this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.                           *
 15→ ****************************************************************************************/
 16→
 17→/**
 18→ * With the Rust backend, operations that modify the collection return a description of changes (OpChanges).
 19→ * The UI can subscribe to these changes, so it can update itself when actions have been performed
 20→ * (eg, the deck list can check if studyQueues has been updated, and if so, it will redraw the list).
 21→ *
 22→ * The optional handler argument can be used so that the initiator of an action can tell when a
 23→ * OpChanges action was caused by itself. This can be useful when the default change behaviour
 24→ * should be ignored, in favour of specific handling (eg the UI wishes to directly update the
 25→ * displayed flag, without redrawing the entire review screen).
 26→ */
 27→
 28→package com.ichi2.libanki
 29→
 30→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 31→import anki.collection.OpChanges
 32→import anki.collection.OpChangesAfterUndo
 33→import anki.collection.OpChangesWithCount
 34→import anki.collection.OpChangesWithId
 35→import anki.import_export.ImportResponse
 36→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.withCol
 37→import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
 38→import kotlinx.coroutines.withContext
 39→import java.lang.ref.WeakReference
 40→
 41→object ChangeManager {
 42→    interface Subscriber {
 43→        /**
 44→         * Called after a backend method invoked via col.op() or col.opWithProgress()
 45→         * has modified the collection. Subscriber should inspect the changes, and update
 46→         * the UI if necessary.
 47→         */
 48→        fun opExecuted(changes: OpChanges, handler: Any?)
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    private val subscribers = mutableListOf<WeakReference<Subscriber>>()
 52→
 53→    fun subscribe(subscriber: Subscriber) {
 54→        subscribers.add(WeakReference(subscriber))
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    private fun notifySubscribers(changes: OpChanges, handler: Any?) {
 58→        val expired = mutableListOf<WeakReference<Subscriber>>()
 59→        for (subscriber in subscribers) {
 60→            val ref = subscriber.get()
 61→            if (ref == null) {
 62→                expired.add(subscriber)
 63→            } else {
 64→                ref.opExecuted(changes, handler)
 65→            }
 66→        }
 67→        for (item in expired) {
 68→            subscribers.remove(item)
 69→        }
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    @VisibleForTesting
 73→    fun clearSubscribers() {
 74→        subscribers.clear()
 75→    }
 76→
 77→    internal fun <T> notifySubscribers(changes: T, initiator: Any?) {
 78→        val opChanges = when (changes) {
 79→            is OpChanges -> changes
 80→            is OpChangesWithCount -> changes.changes
 81→            is OpChangesWithId -> changes.changes
 82→            is OpChangesAfterUndo -> changes.changes
 83→            is ImportResponse -> changes.changes
 84→            else -> TODO("unhandled change type")
 85→        }
 86→        notifySubscribers(opChanges, initiator)
 87→    }
 88→}
 89→
 90→/** Wrap a routine that returns OpChanges* or similar undo info with this
 91→ * to notify change subscribers of the changes. */
 92→suspend fun <T> undoableOp(handler: Any? = null, block: Collection.() -> T): T {
 93→    return withCol {
 94→        block()
 95→    }.also {
 96→        withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
 97→            ChangeManager.notifySubscribers(it, handler)
 98→        }
 99→    }
100→}
101→

34→    fun setCustomButtonsStatus(menu: Menu) {
35→        status.setCustomButtons(menu)
36→        mMenu = menu
37→    }
38→
39→    fun findMenuItem(@IdRes resId: Int) = mMenu?.findItem(resId) as? MenuItemImpl
40→
41→    companion object {
42→        @IdRes
43→        val RES_FLAG = R.id.action_flag
44→
45→        @IdRes
46→        val RES_MARK = R.id.action_mark_card
47→    }
48→}

content.text:
I have implemented the "Redo" option for Answer Cards in the Reviewer, following the behavior of Desktop Anki. I also included support for redoing strokes on the whiteboard, as requested in the issue discussion.

### Changes implemented:

1.  **Collection Logic**: Added `redoLabel()` and `redoAvailable()` to `Collection.kt` to allow checking for redo availability and retrieving the localized action name from the backend.
2.  **Undo/Redo Utilities**: Added `redoAndShowSnackbar()` to `Undo.kt` to handle redoing actions and displaying appropriate feedback messages using backend translations.
3.  **Viewer Commands**:
    *   Added `REDO` to `ViewerCommand` enum.
    *   Added default keyboard bindings for Redo: `Ctrl+Y` and `Ctrl+Shift+Z`.
    *   Implemented Redo command handling in `AbstractFlashcardViewer`.
4.  **Reviewer UI**:
    *   Updated `Reviewer.kt` to handle the Redo menu item and keyboard shortcuts.
    *   Integrated whiteboard redo support: if the whiteboard is visible and has undone strokes, the Redo button redoes those strokes; otherwise, it redoes the last collection operation.
    *   Updated `onCreateOptionsMenu` to dynamically enable/disable the Redo button and set its tooltip/title based on the available redo action.
5.  **Preferences & Customization**:
    *   Added the Redo button to `ActionButtonStatus` to support custom button configurations in the Reviewer.
    *   Added Redo to the reset logic in `CustomButtonsSettingsFragment`.
    *   Included Redo-related preferences in `UsageAnalytics` for better feature tracking.
6.  **UI Components**:
    *   Fixed `RtlCompliantActionProvider` to use the correct menu item ID instead of hardcoding `action_undo`. This ensures that both Undo and Redo buttons work correctly when RTL mirroring is applied.
    *   Created `ic_redo.xml` vector drawable for the Redo icon.
7.  **Whiteboard**:
    *   Implemented `redo()` and `redoEmpty()` in `Whiteboard.kt` to support redoing handwritten strokes.

These changes provide a symmetrical Undo/Redo experience in the Reviewer, reducing frustration from accidental undos.

🤖 Generated with [Firebender](https://firebender.com)

Co-Authored-By: Firebender <help@firebender.com>

27→/** If there's an action pending in the review queue, undo it and show a snackbar */
28→suspend fun FragmentActivity.undoAndShowSnackbar(duration: Int = Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT) {
29→    withProgress {
30→        val changes = undoableOp {
31→            if (!undoAvailable()) {
32→                OpChangesAfterUndo.getDefaultInstance()
33→            } else {
34→                undo()
35→            }
36→        }
37→        val message = if (changes.operation.isEmpty()) {
38→            TR.actionsNothingToUndo()
39→        } else {
40→            TR.undoActionUndone(changes.operation)
41→        }
42→        showSnackbar(message, duration)
43→    }
44→}

 50→// Anki maintains a cache of used tags so it can quickly present a list of tags
 51→// for autocomplete and in the browser. For efficiency, deletions are not
 52→// tracked, so unused tags can only be removed from the list with a DB check.
 53→//
 54→// This module manages the tag cache and tags for notes.
 55→@KotlinCleanup("TextUtils -> Kotlin isNotEmpty()")
 56→@KotlinCleanup("inline function in init { } so we don't need to init `crt` etc... at the definition")
 57→@KotlinCleanup("ids.size != 0")
 58→@WorkerThread
 59→open class Collection(
 60→    /**
 61→     *  @param Path The path to the collection.anki2 database. Must be unicode and openable with [File].
 62→     */
 63→    val path: String,
 64→    private var debugLog: Boolean, // Not in libAnki.
 65→    /**
 66→     * Outside of libanki, you should not access the backend directly for collection operations.
 67→     * Operations that work on a closed collection (eg importing), or do not require a collection
 68→     * at all (eg translations) are the exception.
 69→     */
 70→    val backend: Backend
 71→) {
 72→    /** Access backend translations */
 73→    val tr = backend.tr
 74→
 75→    @get:JvmName("isDbClosed")
 76→    val dbClosed: Boolean
 77→        get() {
 78→            return dbInternal == null
 79→        }
 80→
 81→    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
 82→    fun debugEnsureNoOpenPointers() {
 83→        val result = backend.getActiveSequenceNumbers()
 84→        if (result.isNotEmpty()) {
 85→            val numbers = result.toString()
 86→            throw IllegalStateException("Contained unclosed sequence numbers: $numbers")
 87→        }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    // a lot of legacy code does not check for nullability
 91→    val db: DB
 92→        get() = dbInternal!!
 93→
 94→    var dbInternal: DB? = null
 95→
 96→    /**
 97→     * Getters/Setters ********************************************************** *************************************
 98→     */
 99→
100→    val media: Media
101→
102→    lateinit var decks: Decks
103→        protected set
104→
105→    val tags: Tags
106→

108→        "move accessor methods here, maybe reconsider return type." +
109→            "See variable: conf"
110→    )
111→    lateinit var config: Config
112→
113→    @KotlinCleanup("see if we can inline a function inside init {} and make this `val`")
114→    lateinit var sched: Scheduler
115→        protected set
116→
117→    private var mStartTime: Long
118→    private var mStartReps: Int
119→
120→    var mod: Long = 0
121→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select mod from col")
122→
123→    var crt: Long = 0
124→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select crt from col")
125→

127→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select scm from col")
128→

130→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select ls from col")
131→

133→        return -1
134→    }

136→    var ls: Long = 0
137→    // END: SQL table columns

139→    private var mLogHnd: PrintWriter? = null
140→
141→    init {
142→        media = initMedia()
143→        tags = initTags()
144→        val created = reopen()
145→        log(path, VersionUtils.pkgVersionName)
146→        mStartReps = 0
147→        mStartTime = 0

150→            onCreate()
151→        }
152→    }

155→        return Media(this)

183→    fun schedVer(): Int {
184→        // schedVer was not set on legacy v1 collections
185→        val ver = config.get("schedVer") ?: 1
186→        return if (listOf(1, 2).contains(ver)) {
187→            ver

193→    fun _loadScheduler() {
194→        val ver = schedVer()
195→        if (ver == 1) {
196→            sched = DummyScheduler(this)
197→        } else {
198→            if (!backend.getConfigBool(ConfigKey.Bool.SCHED_2021)) {
199→                backend.setConfigBool(ConfigKey.Bool.SCHED_2021, true, undoable = false)
200→            }
201→            sched = Scheduler(this)
202→            config.set("localOffset", sched._current_timezone_offset())
203→        }
204→    }

209→     * AnkiDroid always saves as changes are made.

211→    @Synchronized
212→    fun close(downgrade: Boolean = false, forFullSync: Boolean = false) {
213→        if (!dbClosed) {
214→            if (!forFullSync) {

224→    fun reopen(afterFullSync: Boolean = false): Boolean {
225→        Timber.i("(Re)opening Database: %s", path)
226→        return if (dbClosed) {
227→            val (db_, created) = Storage.openDB(path, backend, afterFullSync)
228→            dbInternal = db_
229→            load()
230→            _openLog()
231→            if (afterFullSync) {
232→                _loadScheduler()
233→            }
234→            created
235→        } else {
236→            false
237→        }

246→    /** Note: not in libanki.  Mark schema modified to force a full
247→     * sync, but with the confirmation checking function disabled This
248→     * is equivalent to `modSchema(False)` in Anki. A distinct method
249→     * is used so that the type does not states that an exception is
250→     * thrown when in fact it is never thrown.
251→     */
252→    open fun modSchemaNoCheck() {
253→        db.execute(
254→            "update col set scm=?, mod=?",
255→            TimeManager.time.intTimeMS(),
256→            TimeManager.time.intTimeMS()
257→        )

260→    /** Mark schema modified to force a full sync.
261→     * ConfirmModSchemaException will be thrown if the user needs to be prompted to confirm the action.
262→     * If the user chooses to confirm then modSchemaNoCheck should be called, after which the exception can
263→     * be safely ignored, and the outer code called again.
264→     *
265→     * @throws ConfirmModSchemaException
266→     */
267→    @Throws(ConfirmModSchemaException::class)
268→    fun modSchema() {
269→        if (!schemaChanged()) {
270→            /* In Android we can't show a dialog which blocks the main UI thread
271→             Therefore we can't wait for the user to confirm if they want to do
272→             a full sync here, and we instead throw an exception asking the outer
273→             code to handle the user's choice */
274→            throw ConfirmModSchemaException()
275→        }
276→        modSchemaNoCheck()
277→    }

423→        // so not much effort was expended here
424→
425→        val noteIds = findNotes(query, SortOrder.NoOrdering())
426→        // select the card with the lowest `ord` to show
427→        return db.queryLongList(
428→            """
429→SELECT c.id
430→FROM (
431→  SELECT nid, MIN(ord) AS ord
432→  FROM cards
433→  WHERE nid IN ${Utils.ids2str(noteIds)} 
434→  GROUP BY nid
435→) AS card_with_min_ord
436→JOIN cards AS c ON card_with_min_ord.nid = c.nid AND card_with_min_ord.ord = c.ord;
437→            """.trimMargin()
438→        )
439→    }

456→
457→    fun startTimebox() {
458→        mStartTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
459→        mStartReps = sched.reps
460→    }
461→
462→    data class TimeboxReached(val secs: Int, val reps: Int)
463→
464→    /* Return (elapsedTime, reps) if timebox reached, or null.
465→    * Automatically restarts timebox if expired. */
466→    fun timeboxReached(): TimeboxReached? {
467→        if (sched.timeboxSecs() == 0) {
468→            // timeboxing disabled
469→            return null
470→        }
471→        val elapsed = TimeManager.time.intTime() - mStartTime
472→        val limit = sched.timeboxSecs()
473→        return if (elapsed > limit) {
474→            TimeboxReached(
475→                limit,
476→                sched.reps - mStartReps
477→            ).also {
478→                startTimebox()
479→            }
480→        } else {
481→            null
482→        }
483→    }

536→    /*
537→     * Basic integrity check. Only used by unit tests.
538→     */
539→    @KotlinCleanup("have getIds() return a list of mids and define idsToStr over it")
540→    fun basicCheck(): Boolean {
541→        // cards without notes
542→        if (db.queryScalar("select 1 from cards where nid not in (select id from notes) limit 1") > 0) {
543→            return false
544→        }
545→        val badNotes = db.queryScalar(
546→            "select 1 from notes where id not in (select distinct nid from cards) " +
547→                "or mid not in " + Utils.ids2str(notetypes.ids()) + " limit 1"
548→        ) > 0
549→        // notes without cards or models
550→        if (badNotes) {
551→            return false
552→        }
553→        // invalid ords
554→        for (m in notetypes.all()) {
555→            // ignore clozes
556→            if (m.getInt("type") != Consts.MODEL_STD) {
557→                continue
558→            }
559→            // Make a list of valid ords for this model
560→            val tmpls = m.getJSONArray("tmpls")
561→            val badOrd = db.queryScalar(
562→                "select 1 from cards where (ord < 0 or ord >= ?) and nid in ( " +
563→                    "select id from notes where mid = ?) limit 1",
564→                tmpls.length(),
565→                m.getLong("id")
566→            ) > 0
567→            if (badOrd) {
568→                return false
569→            }
570→        }
571→        return true
572→    }

574→    fun log(vararg objects: Any?) {
575→        if (!debugLog) return
576→
577→        val unixTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
578→
579→        val outerTraceElement = Thread.currentThread().stackTrace[3]
580→        val fileName = outerTraceElement.fileName
581→        val methodName = outerTraceElement.methodName
582→
583→        val objectsString = objects
584→            .map { if (it is LongArray) Arrays.toString(it) else it }
585→            .joinToString(", ")
586→
587→        writeLog("[$unixTime] $fileName:$methodName() $objectsString")
588→    }
589→
590→    private fun writeLog(s: String) {
591→        mLogHnd?.let {
592→            try {
593→                it.println(s)
594→            } catch (e: Exception) {
595→                Timber.w(e, "Failed to write to collection log")
596→            }
597→        }
598→        Timber.d(s)
599→    }
600→
601→    private fun _openLog() {
602→        Timber.i("Opening Collection Log")
603→        if (!debugLog) {
604→            return
605→        }
606→        try {
607→            val lpath = File(path.replaceFirst("\\.anki2$".toRegex(), ".log"))
608→            if (lpath.exists() && lpath.length() > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
609→                val lpath2 = File("$lpath.old")
610→                if (lpath2.exists()) {
611→                    lpath2.delete()
612→                }
613→                lpath.renameTo(lpath2)
614→            }
615→            mLogHnd = PrintWriter(BufferedWriter(FileWriter(lpath, true)), true)
616→        } catch (e: IOException) {
617→            // turn off logging if we can't open the log file
618→            Timber.e("Failed to open collection.log file - disabling logging")
619→            debugLog = false
620→        }
621→    }
622→
623→    private fun _closeLog() {
624→        Timber.i("Closing Collection Log")
625→        mLogHnd?.close()
626→        mLogHnd = null
627→    }

23→/**
24→ * If undo/redo available, a localized string describing the action will be set.
25→ */
26→data class UndoStatus(
27→    val undo: String?,
28→    val redo: String?,
29→    // not currently used
30→    val lastStep: Int
31→) {
32→    companion object {
33→        fun from(proto: UndoStatusProto): UndoStatus {
34→            return UndoStatus(
35→                undo = proto.undo.ifEmpty { null },
36→                redo = proto.redo.ifEmpty { null },
37→                lastStep = proto.lastStep
38→            )
39→        }
40→    }
41→}

43→/**
44→ * Undo the last backend operation.
45→ *
46→ * Should be called via collection.op(), which will notify
47→ * [ChangeManager.Subscriber] of the changes.
48→ *
49→ * Will throw if no undo operation is possible (due to legacy code
50→ * directly mutating the database).
51→ */
52→@RustCleanup("Once fully migrated, and v2 scheduler dropped, rename to undo()")
53→fun Collection.undo(): OpChangesAfterUndo {
54→    return backend.undo()
55→}

57→/** Redoes the previously-undone operation. See the docs for
58→[Collection.undoOperation]

60→fun Collection.redo(): OpChangesAfterUndo {
61→    return backend.redo()
62→}

64→/** See [UndoStatus] */
65→fun Collection.undoStatus(): UndoStatus {
66→    return UndoStatus.from(backend.getUndoStatus())

 248→    private fun selectDeckFromExtra() {
 249→        val extras = intent.extras
 250→        if (extras == null || !extras.containsKey("deckId")) {
 251→            // deckId is not set, load default
 252→            return
 253→        }
 254→        val did = extras.getLong("deckId", Long.MIN_VALUE)
 255→        Timber.d("selectDeckFromExtra() with deckId = %d", did)
 256→
 257→        // deckId does not exist, load default
 258→        if (getColUnsafe.decks.get(did) == null) {
 259→            Timber.w("selectDeckFromExtra() deckId '%d' doesn't exist", did)
 260→            return
 261→        }
 262→        // Select the deck
 263→        getColUnsafe.decks.select(did)
 264→    }

 278→    override fun onCollectionLoaded(col: Collection) {
 279→        super.onCollectionLoaded(col)
 280→        if (Intent.ACTION_VIEW == intent.action) {
 281→            Timber.d("onCreate() :: received Intent with action = %s", intent.action)
 282→            selectDeckFromExtra()
 283→        }
 284→        // Load the first card and start reviewing. Uses the answer card
 285→        // task to load a card, but since we send null
 286→        // as the card to answer, no card will be answered.
 287→        prefWhiteboard = MetaDB.getWhiteboardState(this, parentDid)
 288→        if (prefWhiteboard) {
 289→            // DEFECT: Slight inefficiency here, as we set the database using these methods
 290→            val whiteboardVisibility = MetaDB.getWhiteboardVisibility(this, parentDid)
 291→            setWhiteboardEnabledState(true)
 292→            setWhiteboardVisibility(whiteboardVisibility)
 293→            toggleStylus = MetaDB.getWhiteboardStylusState(this, parentDid)
 294→            whiteboard!!.toggleStylus = toggleStylus
 295→        }
 296→        launchCatchingTask { updateCardAndRedraw() }
 297→        disableDrawerSwipeOnConflicts()
 298→
 299→        // Set full screen/immersive mode if needed
 300→        if (mPrefFullscreenReview) {
 301→            setFullScreen(this)
 302→        }
 303→        setRenderWorkaround(this)
 304→    }

 306→    override fun onOptionsItemSelected(item: MenuItem): Boolean {
 307→        // 100ms was not enough on my device (Honor 9 Lite -  Android Pie)
 308→        delayedHide(1000)
 309→        if (drawerToggle.onOptionsItemSelected(item)) {
 310→            return true
 311→        }
 312→        when (item.itemId) {
 313→            android.R.id.home -> {
 314→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Home button pressed")
 315→                closeReviewer(RESULT_OK)
 316→            }
 317→            R.id.action_undo -> {
 318→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Undo button pressed")
 319→                if (mShowWhiteboard && whiteboard != null && !whiteboard!!.undoEmpty()) {
 320→                    whiteboard!!.undo()
 321→                } else {

 553→    private fun openOrToggleMicToolbar() {
 554→        if (!canRecordAudio(this)) {
 555→            ActivityCompat.requestPermissions(
 556→                this,
 557→                arrayOf(Manifest.permission.RECORD_AUDIO),
 558→                REQUEST_AUDIO_PERMISSION
 559→            )
 560→        } else {
 561→            toggleMicToolBar()
 562→        }
 563→    }
 564→
 565→    private fun toggleMicToolBar() {
 566→        audioView?.let {
 567→            it.visibility = if (it.visibility != View.VISIBLE) View.VISIBLE else View.GONE
 568→            return
 569→        }
 570→        // Record mic tool bar does not exist yet
 571→        tempAudioPath = generateTempAudioFile(this)
 572→        if (tempAudioPath == null) {
 573→            return
 574→        }
 575→        audioView = createRecorderInstance(
 576→            this,
 577→            R.drawable.ic_play_arrow_white_24dp,
 578→            R.drawable.ic_pause_white_24dp,
 579→            R.drawable.ic_stop_white_24dp,
 580→            R.drawable.ic_rec,
 581→            R.drawable.ic_rec_stop,
 582→            tempAudioPath!!
 583→        )
 584→        if (audioView == null) {
 585→            tempAudioPath = null
 586→            return
 587→        }
 588→        val lp2 = FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
 589→            ViewGroup.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
 590→            ViewGroup.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
 591→        )
 592→        audioView!!.layoutParams = lp2
 593→        val micToolBarLayer = findViewById<LinearLayout>(R.id.mic_tool_bar_layer)
 594→        micToolBarLayer.addView(audioView)
 595→    }

 637→    @NeedsTest("Starting animation from swipe is inverse to the finishing one")
 638→    private fun addNote(fromGesture: Gesture? = null) {
 639→        val intent = Intent(this, NoteEditor::class.java)
 640→        val animation = getAnimationTransitionFromGesture(fromGesture)
 641→        intent.putExtra(NoteEditor.EXTRA_CALLER, NoteEditor.CALLER_REVIEWER_ADD)
 642→        intent.putExtra(FINISH_ANIMATION_EXTRA, getInverseTransition(animation) as Parcelable)
 643→        startActivityForResultWithAnimation(intent, ADD_NOTE, animation)
 644→    }

 646→    @NeedsTest("Starting animation from swipe is inverse to the finishing one")
 647→    protected fun openCardInfo(fromGesture: Gesture? = null) {
 648→        if (currentCard == null) {
 649→            showSnackbar(getString(R.string.multimedia_editor_something_wrong), Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT)
 650→            return
 651→        }
 652→        val intent = com.ichi2.anki.pages.CardInfo.getIntent(this, currentCard!!.id)
 653→        val animation = getAnimationTransitionFromGesture(fromGesture)
 654→        intent.putExtra(FINISH_ANIMATION_EXTRA, getInverseTransition(animation) as Parcelable)
 655→        startActivityWithAnimation(intent, animation)
 656→    }

 658→    // Related to https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/11061#issuecomment-1107868455
 659→    @NeedsTest("Order of operations needs Testing around Menu (Overflow) Icons and their colors.")
 660→    override fun onCreateOptionsMenu(menu: Menu): Boolean {
 661→        Timber.d("onCreateOptionsMenu()")
 662→        // NOTE: This is called every time a new question is shown via invalidate options menu
 663→        menuInflater.inflate(R.menu.reviewer, menu)
 664→        displayIcons(menu)
 665→        mActionButtons.setCustomButtonsStatus(menu)
 666→        var alpha = if (super.controlBlocked !== ReviewerUi.ControlBlock.SLOW) Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT else Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 667→        val markCardIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_mark_card)
 668→        if (currentCard != null && isMarked(currentCard!!.note())) {
 669→            markCardIcon.setTitle(R.string.menu_unmark_note).setIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_white)
 670→        } else {

 680→                    2 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_orange)
 681→                    3 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_green)
 682→                    4 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_blue)
 683→                    5 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_pink)
 684→                    6 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_turquoise)
 685→                    7 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_purple)
 686→                    else -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_transparent)

 691→
 692→        // Undo button
 693→        @DrawableRes val undoIconId: Int
 694→        val undoEnabled: Boolean
 695→        val whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes = mShowWhiteboard && whiteboard?.undoEmpty() == false
 696→        if (whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes) {
 697→            undoIconId = R.drawable.eraser

 699→        } else {
 700→            undoIconId = R.drawable.ic_undo_white

 702→        }
 703→        val alphaUndo = if (undoEnabled && super.controlBlocked !== ReviewerUi.ControlBlock.SLOW) Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT else Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 704→        val undoIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_undo)

 706→        undoIcon.setEnabled(undoEnabled).iconAlpha = alphaUndo
 707→        undoIcon.actionView!!.isEnabled = undoEnabled

 709→            if (whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes) {

 711→            } else if (getColUnsafe.undoAvailable()) {

 714→            } else {
 715→                // In this case, there is no object word for the verb, "Undo",
 716→                // so in some languages such as Japanese, which have pre/post-positional particle with the object,
 717→                // we need to use the string for just "Undo" instead of the string for "Undo %s".
 718→                undoIcon.title = resources.getString(R.string.undo)

 936→    private fun updateScreenCounts() {
 937→        val queue = queueState ?: return
 938→        super.updateActionBar()
 939→        val actionBar = supportActionBar
 940→        val counts = queue.counts
 941→        if (actionBar != null) {
 942→            if (mPrefShowETA) {
 943→                launchCatchingTask {
 944→                    mEta = withCol { sched.eta(counts, false) }
 945→                    actionBar.subtitle = remainingTime(this@Reviewer, (mEta * 60).toLong())
 946→                }
 947→            }
 948→        }
 949→        mNewCount = SpannableString(counts.new.toString())
 950→        mLrnCount = SpannableString(counts.lrn.toString())
 951→        mRevCount = SpannableString(counts.rev.toString())
 952→        if (mPrefHideDueCount) {
 953→            mRevCount = SpannableString("???")
 954→        }
 955→        // if this code is run as a card is being answered, currentCard may be non-null but
 956→        // the queues may be empty - we can't call countIdx() in such a case
 957→        if (counts.count() != 0) {
 958→            when (queue.countsIndex) {
 959→                Counts.Queue.NEW -> mNewCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mNewCount!!.length, 0)
 960→                Counts.Queue.LRN -> mLrnCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mLrnCount!!.length, 0)
 961→                Counts.Queue.REV -> mRevCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mRevCount!!.length, 0)
 962→            }
 963→        }
 964→        mTextBarNew.text = mNewCount
 965→        mTextBarLearn.text = mLrnCount
 966→        mTextBarReview.text = mRevCount
 967→    }

 985→    override suspend fun updateCurrentCard() {

 987→            sched.currentQueueState()?.apply {
 988→                topCard.renderOutput(true)
 989→            }
 990→        }
 991→        state?.timeboxReached?.let { dealWithTimeBox(it) }
 992→        currentCard = state?.topCard
 993→        queueState = state

 996→    override suspend fun answerCardInner(ease: Int) {
 997→        val state = queueState!!
 998→        Timber.d("answerCardInner: ${currentCard!!.id} $ease")
 999→        var wasLeech = false
1000→        undoableOp(this) {
1001→            sched.answerCard(state, ease).also {
1002→                wasLeech = sched.againIsLeech(state)
1003→            }
1004→        }.also {
1005→            if (ease == Consts.BUTTON_ONE && wasLeech) {
1006→                state.topCard.load()
1007→                val leechMessage: String = if (state.topCard.queue < 0) {
1008→                    resources.getString(R.string.leech_suspend_notification)
1009→                } else {
1010→                    resources.getString(R.string.leech_notification)
1011→                }
1012→                showSnackbar(leechMessage, Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT)
1013→            }
1014→        }
1015→    }

1017→    private fun dealWithTimeBox(timebox: Collection.TimeboxReached) {
1018→        val nCards = timebox.reps
1019→        val nMins = timebox.secs / 60
1020→        val mins = resources.getQuantityString(R.plurals.in_minutes, nMins, nMins)
1021→        val timeboxMessage = resources.getQuantityString(R.plurals.timebox_reached, nCards, nCards, mins)
1022→        AlertDialog.Builder(this).show {
1023→            title(R.string.timebox_reached_title)
1024→            message(text = timeboxMessage)
1025→            positiveButton(R.string.dialog_continue) {}
1026→            negativeButton(text = CollectionManager.TR.studyingFinish()) {
1027→                finishWithAnimation(ActivityTransitionAnimation.Direction.END)
1028→            }
1029→            cancelable(true)
1030→            setOnCancelListener { }
1031→        }
1032→    }

1047→    private fun runStateMutationHook() {
1048→        val state = queueState ?: return
1049→        if (state.customSchedulingJs.isEmpty()) {
1050→            return
1051→        }
1052→        val key = customSchedulingKey
1053→        val js = state.customSchedulingJs
1054→        webView?.evaluateJavascript(
1055→            """
1056→        anki.mutateNextCardStates('$key', async (states, customData, ctx) => {{ $js }})
1057→            .catch(err => console.log(err));
1058→"""
1059→        ) {}
1060→    }

1105→    override fun executeCommand(which: ViewerCommand, fromGesture: Gesture?): Boolean {
1106→        if (isControlBlocked && which !== ViewerCommand.EXIT) {
1107→            return false
1108→        }
1109→        when (which) {
1110→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> {
1111→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_RED)
1112→                return true
1113→            }
1114→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> {
1115→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_ORANGE)
1116→                return true
1117→            }
1118→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> {
1119→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_GREEN)
1120→                return true
1121→            }
1122→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> {
1123→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_BLUE)
1124→                return true
1125→            }
1126→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> {
1127→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_PINK)
1128→                return true
1129→            }
1130→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> {
1131→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_TURQUOISE)
1132→                return true
1133→            }
1134→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> {
1135→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_PURPLE)
1136→                return true
1137→            }
1138→            ViewerCommand.UNSET_FLAG -> {
1139→                onFlag(currentCard, CardMarker.FLAG_NONE)
1140→                return true
1141→            }
1142→            ViewerCommand.MARK -> {
1143→                onMark(currentCard)
1144→                return true
1145→            }
1146→            ViewerCommand.ADD_NOTE -> {
1147→                addNote(fromGesture)
1148→                return true
1149→            }
1150→            ViewerCommand.CARD_INFO -> {
1151→                openCardInfo(fromGesture)
1152→                return true
1153→            }
1154→            ViewerCommand.RESCHEDULE_NOTE -> {
1155→                showRescheduleCardDialog()
1156→                return true
1157→            }
1158→            else -> return super.executeCommand(which, fromGesture)
1159→        }
1160→    }

1166→        } else {
1167→            Timber.i("Toggle flag: Setting flag to %d", flag)
1168→            onFlag(currentCard, flag)
1169→        }
1170→    }

1226→    @Suppress("deprecation") // #9332: UI Visibility -> Insets
1227→    private fun setFullScreen(a: AbstractFlashcardViewer) {
1228→        // Set appropriate flags to enable Sticky Immersive mode.
1229→        a.window.decorView.systemUiVisibility = (
1230→            View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_STABLE // | View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_HIDE_NAVIGATION // temporarily disabled due to #5245
1231→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_FULLSCREEN
1232→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_HIDE_NAVIGATION
1233→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_FULLSCREEN
1234→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LOW_PROFILE
1235→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_IMMERSIVE
1236→            )
1237→        // Show / hide the Action bar together with the status bar
1238→        val prefs = a.sharedPrefs()
1239→        val fullscreenMode = fromPreference(prefs)
1240→        a.window.statusBarColor = MaterialColors.getColor(a, R.attr.appBarColor, 0)
1241→        val decorView = a.window.decorView
1242→        decorView.setOnSystemUiVisibilityChangeListener { flags: Int ->
1243→            val toolbar = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.toolbar)
1244→            val answerButtons = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.answer_options_layout)
1245→            val topbar = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.top_bar)
1246→            if (toolbar == null || topbar == null || answerButtons == null) {
1247→                return@setOnSystemUiVisibilityChangeListener
1248→            }
1249→            // Note that system bars will only be "visible" if none of the
1250→            // LOW_PROFILE, HIDE_NAVIGATION, or FULLSCREEN flags are set.
1251→            val visible = flags and View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_HIDE_NAVIGATION == 0
1252→            Timber.d("System UI visibility change. Visible: %b", visible)
1253→            if (visible) {
1254→                showViewWithAnimation(toolbar)
1255→                if (fullscreenMode == FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE) {
1256→                    showViewWithAnimation(topbar)
1257→                    showViewWithAnimation(answerButtons)
1258→                }
1259→            } else {
1260→                hideViewWithAnimation(toolbar)
1261→                if (fullscreenMode == FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE) {
1262→                    hideViewWithAnimation(topbar)
1263→                    hideViewWithAnimation(answerButtons)
1264→                }
1265→            }
1266→        }
1267→    }

1269→    private fun showViewWithAnimation(view: View) {
1270→        view.alpha = 0.0f
1271→        view.visibility = View.VISIBLE
1272→        view.animate().alpha(TRANSPARENCY).setDuration(ANIMATION_DURATION.toLong()).setListener(null)
1273→    }
1274→
1275→    private fun hideViewWithAnimation(view: View) {
1276→        view.animate()
1277→            .alpha(0f)
1278→            .setDuration(ANIMATION_DURATION.toLong())
1279→            .setListener(object : AnimatorListenerAdapter() {
1280→                override fun onAnimationEnd(animation: Animator) {
1281→                    view.visibility = View.GONE
1282→                }
1283→            })
1284→    }

1291→    private fun createWhiteboard() {
1292→        whiteboard = createInstance(this, true, this)
1293→
1294→        // We use the pen color of the selected deck at the time the whiteboard is enabled.
1295→        // This is how all other whiteboard settings are
1296→        val whiteboardPenColor = MetaDB.getWhiteboardPenColor(this, parentDid).fromPreferences()
1297→        if (whiteboardPenColor != null) {
1298→            whiteboard!!.penColor = whiteboardPenColor
1299→        }
1300→        whiteboard!!.setOnPaintColorChangeListener(object : OnPaintColorChangeListener {
1301→            override fun onPaintColorChange(color: Int?) {
1302→                MetaDB.storeWhiteboardPenColor(this@Reviewer, parentDid, !currentTheme.isNightMode, color)
1303→            }
1304→        })
1305→        whiteboard!!.setOnTouchListener { v: View, event: MotionEvent? ->
1306→            if (event == null) return@setOnTouchListener false
1307→            // If the whiteboard is currently drawing, and triggers the system UI to show, we want to continue drawing.
1308→            if (!whiteboard!!.isCurrentlyDrawing && (
1309→                !mShowWhiteboard || (
1310→                    mPrefFullscreenReview &&
1311→                        isImmersiveSystemUiVisible(this@Reviewer)
1312→                    )
1313→                )
1314→            ) {
1315→                // Bypass whiteboard listener when it's hidden or fullscreen immersive mode is temporarily suspended
1316→                v.performClick()
1317→                return@setOnTouchListener gestureDetector!!.onTouchEvent(event)
1318→            }
1319→            whiteboard!!.handleTouchEvent(event)
1320→        }
1321→    }
1322→

1329→            disableDrawerSwipe()

1362→    @KotlinCleanup("mCurrentCard handling")
1363→    private fun suspendNoteAvailable(): Boolean {
1364→        return if (currentCard == null || isControlBlocked) {
1365→            false
1366→        } else {
1367→            getColUnsafe.db.queryScalar(
1368→                "select 1 from cards where nid = ? and id != ? and queue != " + Consts.QUEUE_TYPE_SUSPENDED + " limit 1",
1369→                currentCard!!.nid,
1370→                currentCard!!.id
1371→            ) == 1
1372→        }
1373→        // whether there exists a sibling not buried.
1374→    }

1377→    private fun buryNoteAvailable(): Boolean {

1380→        } else {
1381→            getColUnsafe.db.queryScalar(
1382→                "select 1 from cards where nid = ? and id != ? and queue >=  " + Consts.QUEUE_TYPE_NEW + " limit 1",
1383→                currentCard!!.nid,
1384→                currentCard!!.id
1385→            ) == 1
1386→        }
1387→        // Whether there exists a sibling which is neither suspended nor buried
1388→    }

19→import androidx.fragment.app.FragmentActivity
20→import anki.collection.OpChangesAfterUndo
21→import com.google.android.material.snackbar.Snackbar

23→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
24→import com.ichi2.libanki.undo
25→import com.ichi2.libanki.undoableOp
26→
27→/** If there's an action pending in the review queue, undo it and show a snackbar */
28→suspend fun FragmentActivity.undoAndShowSnackbar(duration: Int = Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT) {
29→    withProgress {
30→        val changes = undoableOp {
31→            if (!undoAvailable()) {
32→                OpChangesAfterUndo.getDefaultInstance()
33→            } else {
34→                undo()
35→            }
36→        }
37→        val message = if (changes.operation.isEmpty()) {
38→            TR.actionsNothingToUndo()
39→        } else {
40→            TR.undoActionUndone(changes.operation)
41→        }
42→        showSnackbar(message, duration)
43→    }
44→}

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.reviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 20→import android.view.Menu
 21→import android.view.MenuItem
 22→import androidx.annotation.IdRes
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 24→import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
 25→import com.ichi2.utils.HashUtil.hashMapInit
 26→
 27→// loads of unboxing issues, which are safe
 28→class ActionButtonStatus(private val reviewerUi: ReviewerUi) {
 29→    /**
 30→     * Custom button allocation
 31→     */
 32→    private val mCustomButtons: MutableMap<Int, Int> = hashMapInit(25) // setup's size
 33→
 34→    fun setup(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
 35→        // NOTE: the default values below should be in sync with preferences_custom_buttons.xml and reviewer.xml
 36→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_undo, "customButtonUndo", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 37→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_schedule, "customButtonScheduleCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 38→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_flag, "customButtonFlag", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 39→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_tag, "customButtonTags", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 40→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_edit, "customButtonEditCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 41→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_add_note_reviewer, "customButtonAddCard", MENU_DISABLED)
 42→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_replay, "customButtonReplay", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 43→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_card_info, "customButtonCardInfo", MENU_DISABLED)
 44→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_clear_whiteboard, "customButtonClearWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 45→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_hide_whiteboard, "customButtonShowHideWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 46→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_select_tts, "customButtonSelectTts", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 47→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_open_deck_options, "customButtonDeckOptions", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 48→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_bury, "customButtonBury", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 49→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_suspend, "customButtonSuspend", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 50→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_mark_card, "customButtonMarkCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 51→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_delete, "customButtonDelete", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 52→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_mic_tool_bar, "customButtonToggleMicToolBar", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 53→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_whiteboard, "customButtonEnableWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 54→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_stylus, "customButtonToggleStylus", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 55→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_save_whiteboard, "customButtonSaveWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 56→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color, "customButtonWhiteboardPenColor", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    private fun setupButton(preferences: SharedPreferences, @IdRes resourceId: Int, preferenceName: String, showAsActionType: Int) {
 60→        mCustomButtons[resourceId] = preferences.getString(
 61→            preferenceName,
 62→            showAsActionType.toString()
 63→        )!!.toInt()
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    fun setCustomButtons(menu: Menu) {
 67→        for ((itemId, value) in mCustomButtons) {
 68→            if (value != MENU_DISABLED) {
 69→                val item = menu.findItem(itemId)
 70→                item.setShowAsAction(value)
 71→                val icon = item.icon
 72→                item.isEnabled = !reviewerUi.isControlBlocked
 73→                if (icon != null) {
 74→                    /* Ideally, we want to give feedback to users that
 75→                    buttons are disabled.  However, some actions are
 76→                    expected to be so quick that the visual feedback
 77→                    is useless and is only seen as a flickering.
 78→
 79→                    We use a heuristic to decide whether the next card
 80→                    will appear quickly or slowly.  We change the
 81→                    color only if the buttons are blocked and we
 82→                    expect the next card to take time to arrive.
 83→                    */
 84→                    val mutableIcon = icon.mutate()
 85→                    if (reviewerUi.controlBlocked == ReviewerUi.ControlBlock.SLOW) {
 86→                        mutableIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 87→                    } else {
 88→                        mutableIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 89→                    }
 90→                }
 91→            } else {
 92→                menu.findItem(itemId).isVisible = false
 93→            }
 94→        }
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    fun hideWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 98→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_hide_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
 99→    }
100→
101→    fun toggleStylusIsDisabled(): Boolean {
102→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_toggle_stylus] == MENU_DISABLED
103→    }
104→
105→    fun clearWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
106→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_clear_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
107→    }
108→
109→    fun selectTtsIsDisabled(): Boolean {
110→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_select_tts] == MENU_DISABLED
111→    }
112→
113→    fun saveWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
114→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_save_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
115→    }
116→
117→    fun whiteboardPenColorIsDisabled(): Boolean {
118→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color] == MENU_DISABLED
119→    }
120→
121→    companion object {
122→        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER
123→        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM
124→        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS
125→        const val MENU_DISABLED = 3
126→    }
127→}

 18→<!-- Custom buttons Preferences -->
 19→<!--
 20→MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER = 0
 21→MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM = 1
 22→MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS  = 2
 23→MENU_DISABLED = 3
 24→
 25→Ensure to add the custom icons to Preferences.java's reset functionality
 26→TODO: Add a unit test
 27→-->
 28→<PreferenceScreen xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
 29→    xmlns:app="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res-auto"
 30→    android:title="@string/custom_buttons"
 31→    android:key="@string/pref_app_bar_buttons_screen_key">
 32→    <Preference
 33→        android:key="@string/reset_custom_buttons_key"
 34→        android:title="@string/reset_custom_buttons" />
 35→    <PreferenceCategory android:title="@string/deck_conf_general" >
 36→        <ListPreference
 37→            android:defaultValue="2"
 38→            android:entries="@array/custom_button_labels"
 39→            android:entryValues="@array/custom_button_values"
 40→            android:key="@string/custom_button_undo_key"
 41→            android:title="@string/undo"
 42→            app:useSimpleSummaryProvider="true"/>
 43→        <ListPreference
 44→            android:defaultValue="1"
 45→            android:entries="@array/custom_button_labels"
 46→            android:entryValues="@array/custom_button_values"
 47→            android:key="@string/custom_button_redo_key"
 48→            android:title="@string/redo"
 49→            app:useSimpleSummaryProvider="true"/>
 50→        <ListPreference
 51→            android:defaultValue="0"
 52→            android:entries="@array/custom_button_labels"
 53→            android:entryValues="@array/custom_button_values"
 54→            android:key="@string/custom_button_schedule_card_key"
 55→            android:title="@string/card_editor_reschedule_card"
 56→            app:useSimpleSummaryProvider="true"/>
 57→        <ListPreference

 36→enum class ViewerCommand(val resourceId: Int) {
 37→    SHOW_ANSWER(R.string.show_answer),
 38→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1(R.string.answer_again),
 39→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2(R.string.answer_hard),
 40→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3(R.string.answer_good),
 41→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4(R.string.answer_easy),
 42→    UNDO(R.string.undo),
 43→    EDIT(R.string.cardeditor_title_edit_card),
 44→    MARK(R.string.menu_mark_note),
 45→    BURY_CARD(R.string.menu_bury_card),
 46→    SUSPEND_CARD(R.string.menu_suspend_card),
 47→    DELETE(R.string.menu_delete_note),
 48→    PLAY_MEDIA(R.string.gesture_play),
 49→    EXIT(R.string.gesture_abort_learning),
 50→    BURY_NOTE(R.string.menu_bury_note),
 51→    SUSPEND_NOTE(R.string.menu_suspend_note),
 52→    TOGGLE_FLAG_RED(R.string.gesture_flag_red),
 53→    TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE(R.string.gesture_flag_orange),
 54→    TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN(R.string.gesture_flag_green),
 55→    TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE(R.string.gesture_flag_blue),
 56→    TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK(R.string.gesture_flag_pink),
 57→    TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE(R.string.gesture_flag_turquoise),
 58→    TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE(R.string.gesture_flag_purple),
 59→    UNSET_FLAG(R.string.gesture_flag_remove),
 60→    PAGE_UP(R.string.gesture_page_up),
 61→    PAGE_DOWN(R.string.gesture_page_down),
 62→    TAG(R.string.add_tag),
 63→    CARD_INFO(R.string.card_info_title),
 64→    ABORT_AND_SYNC(R.string.gesture_abort_sync),
 65→    RECORD_VOICE(R.string.record_voice),
 66→    REPLAY_VOICE(R.string.replay_voice),
 67→    TOGGLE_WHITEBOARD(R.string.gesture_toggle_whiteboard),
 68→    CLEAR_WHITEBOARD(R.string.clear_whiteboard),
 69→    CHANGE_WHITEBOARD_PEN_COLOR(R.string.title_whiteboard_editor),
 70→    SHOW_HINT(R.string.gesture_show_hint),
 71→    SHOW_ALL_HINTS(R.string.gesture_show_all_hints),
 72→    ADD_NOTE(R.string.menu_add_note),
 73→    RESCHEDULE_NOTE(R.string.card_editor_reschedule_card);
 74→
 75→    companion object {
 76→        val allDefaultBindings: List<MappableBinding>
 77→            get() = Arrays.stream(values())
 78→                .flatMap { x: ViewerCommand -> x.defaultValue.stream() }
 79→                .collect(Collectors.toList())
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    val preferenceKey: String
 83→        get() = "binding_$name"
 84→
 85→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 86→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 87→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 88→            collection.remove(element)
 89→            collection.add(0, element)
 90→            true
 91→        }
 92→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 96→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 97→            // do not reorder the elements
 98→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
 99→                return@BiFunction false
100→            }
101→            collection.add(element)
102→            return@BiFunction true
103→        }
104→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
108→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
109→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
110→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
111→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
112→    }
113→
114→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
115→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
116→        bindings.remove(binding)
117→        prefs.edit {
118→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
123→    val defaultValue: List<MappableBinding>
124→        get() = // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
125→            when (this) {
126→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1 -> from(
127→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_Y, CardSide.BOTH),
128→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.ANSWER),
129→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.ANSWER)
130→                )
131→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2 -> from(
132→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_X, CardSide.BOTH),
133→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.ANSWER),
134→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.ANSWER)
135→                )
136→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3 -> from(
137→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_B, CardSide.BOTH),
138→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
139→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
140→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
141→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
142→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
143→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
144→                )
145→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> from(
146→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
147→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
148→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
149→                )
150→                EDIT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
151→                MARK -> from(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
152→                BURY_CARD -> from(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
153→                BURY_NOTE -> from(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
154→                SUSPEND_CARD -> from(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
155→                SUSPEND_NOTE -> from(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
156→                PLAY_MEDIA -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
157→                REPLAY_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
158→                RECORD_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
159→                UNDO -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
160→                TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
161→                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
162→                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
163→                TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
164→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
165→                TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
166→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
167→                SHOW_HINT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_H, CardSide.BOTH))
168→                SHOW_ALL_HINTS -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_G, CardSide.BOTH))
169→                ADD_NOTE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_A, CardSide.BOTH))
170→                else -> ArrayList()
171→            }
172→
173→    private fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, keys: ModifierKeys): MappableBinding {
174→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
175→    }
176→
177→    private fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
178→        return MappableBinding(unicode(c), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun from(vararg bindings: MappableBinding): List<MappableBinding> {
182→        return ArrayList(listOf(*bindings))
183→    }
184→
185→    private fun keyCode(keyCode: Int, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
186→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keyCode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
187→    }

191→         *
192→         * example failure: answering an ease on the front of the card
193→         */
194→        fun executeCommand(which: ViewerCommand, fromGesture: Gesture?): Boolean
